races.race_name: Education Network Handicap

runners.name: Caviar Cowboy
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo; won well at Lingfield (7f, AW) in April and then bumped into a useful rival at Kempton last month; 4lb higher and up in class but still has some untapped potential.
runners.comment: Improved RPR with each run; conditions should suit and he gets in here off a light weight

runners.name: Bobby Bennu
runners.spotlight: Winless last season but ran some promising races and did likewise when fourth on his reappearance at Chester (7f, good; Supido third), having been dropped in from a wide draw; continues to drop down the weights; chance.
runners.comment: Promising return at Chester when poorly drawn; good mark on 2025 best

runners.name: Sir Albert
runners.spotlight: Three wins as a 2yo; encouraging return to action at Lingfield in February but neither handicap run since suggests he is the answer today.
runners.comment: Useful 2yo; down the field in handicaps on last two starts; others look stronger

runners.name: Witch Hunter
runners.spotlight: Now 5lb lower than for his 1m win here in January and there have been excuses for several of his defeats since; plausible outsider.
runners.comment: 1m win here in January came off 5lb higher; not much gone right since but revival possible

runners.name: Supido
runners.spotlight: Good effort to win a 7.6f handicap at Chester on his reappearance and he ran to a similar level when third back there (7f) four weeks ago; today's stiffer test at the trip will suit and he has shown promise on AW.
runners.comment: Two solid runs at Chester this season; stiff 7f will suit and he handles AW; respected

runners.name: Yorkshire
runners.spotlight: Four wins and a second from his seven AW runs; stiff task in the Victoria Cup on his return from 287 days off; hopes pinned on the return to AW sparking a full revival.
runners.comment: Good record on AW; stiff task at Ascot seven weeks ago; this should be more suitable

runners.name: Rajeko
runners.spotlight: Only had three runs in the last 15 months and he hasn't beaten a rival in any of them; drops back in trip having been gelded; risky.
runners.comment: Only seen three times in 15 months and he hasn't beaten a rival in any of them; now gelded

runners.name: Sergeant Wilko
runners.spotlight: Winless since his productive 2024 campaign; upped to 7f at Thirsk six weeks ago and put in a good front-running shift, picked off  close home by the reopposing Brighton Boy; 3lb better off today but it won't be easy trying to boss this field.
runners.comment: Fine second upped to 7f at Thirsk six weeks ago; 3lb better off with Brighton Boy today

runners.name: Brighton Boy
runners.spotlight: Led late when beating Sergeant Wilko at Thirsk last month and he had a few of today's rivals in behind when second at Chester four weeks ago; another solid effort at York a fortnight ago but he has to prove his effectiveness on AW.
runners.comment: Arrives in good form and ties in with a few of today's rivals; more to prove on AW

runners.name: Tremolo
runners.spotlight: Found a jolt of improvement last summer, winning handicaps at Kempton (7f) and Southwell (1m) before a good third here (1m) in his hat-trick bid 310 days ago; absent since but he should have more to offer after just eight starts; betting instructive.
runners.comment: On the up last summer, winning twice on AW; still low mileage; betting useful after layoff

runners.name: Nikovo
runners.spotlight: Well suited by conditions and his last-gasp C&D win in March came off just 2lb lower; creditable efforts off his lower turf mark more recently; may find a few of his younger rivals better treated.
runners.comment: Conditions to suit but he's fairly exposed and minor money perhaps his best hope

runners.name: Metal Merchant
runners.spotlight: Smart handicap form for Jack Channon in 2024; well beaten in two runs last year and no sign of a revival in two runs for Brian Toomey this year; starts out for John Butler off a much-reduced mark; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Regressed since mid-2024 but starts out for John Butler off a much-reduced mark

runners.name: Room Service
runners.spotlight: Group placed over 7f last June but not so good afterwards in 2025; sold out of Kevin Ryan's yard for 72,000gns and gelded since last run; wears first-time tongue-tie and blinkers; stiff task on stable/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Smart at best for K Ryan; sold 72,000gns and gelded since last seen; new accessories tried

runners.name: Korker
runners.spotlight: Slow starter; on a long losing run; creditable effort at Chester four weeks ago but this stiff 7f isn't sure to suit.
runners.comment: Habitual slow starter who is on a long losing run; stiff 7f not sure to suit
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing,Mrs Burke & C Wood

races.race_name: Jenningsbet Chipchase Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Marvelman
runners.spotlight: 1-1 on AW, namely a 7f novice a Kempton last March; recorded a big personal best when winning the Group 2 Park Stakes at Doncaster (7f, soft) and his two defeats since have been excusable; tough task trying to give weight away back sprinting after 133 days off.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner (7f, soft) last September; excuses since; tough task under penalty

runners.name: Noble Champion
runners.spotlight: 7f AW win as a 2yo; enjoyed his finest hour when winning the Jersey Stakes at Royal Ascot (7f, good to firm) 12 months ago; only seen twice since, pulling up at Goodwood last July and then finishing a remote last of five in a French Group 3 (soft ground) last month; not solid but he'll be a danger to all if back near that Jersey Stakes form.
runners.comment: Royal Ascot win (7f, Group 3) last June; struggled in both subsequent runs; 1-1 on AW

runners.name: Symbol Of Honour
runners.spotlight: Useful on turf, recording peak RPR when landing a Group 2 at Haydock (6f, good) last May; not at his best in two runs in Dubai this winter; switch to AW shouldn't be an issue and he has the ability to feature.
runners.comment: 5-14 on turf, including Group 2; not at best in Dubai this winter; AW debut

runners.name: Paborus
runners.spotlight: Ended 2024 with a runaway win in a 7f handicap at Southwell; created another big impression when winning a conditions event at Thirsk (7f, good) on last season's reappearance and he was a good third of eight upped to Group 3 company when last seen a year ago; drops to 6f for the first time but this strong traveller should have the pace to cope; still has potential.
runners.comment: Good record fresh; drops in trip but still has potential and a big run seems likely

runners.name: Diligent Harry
runners.spotlight: Made all to win the corresponding race 12 months ago; 2-2 on AW this year and he has performed with credit in Group 3/2 company on turf on his last two starts; should be in the thick of it again.
runners.comment: Fine performer on AW (10-18); arrives in form; bold bid expected from last year's winner

runners.name: Poet Master
runners.spotlight: Won a Group 2 in Ireland in July 2024; not added to that success since but he has run well in two of his three starts this time round, latterly finishing third of eight in a Group 3 at Epsom (7f, good to soft); drops in trip for his AW debut.
runners.comment: Group 2 win in July 2024; in fair form this year but drops in trip today; AW debut
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Bloodsweatandtyres
runners.spotlight: 7f novice win here in November but last of 22 on handicap debut at York (7f, good) five weeks ago; stones to find in this company.
runners.comment: BHA rating of 76 highlights the enormity of his task at this level

runners.name: Heathcliff
runners.spotlight: Wouldn't judge him harshly on his two turf defeats this summer but even his best AW form needs bettering if he is to come out on top here.
runners.comment: Return to AW in his favour but even best efforts lag behind the necessary standard here

runners.name: Wiltshire
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery prior to an impressive performance over C&D on Good Friday; struggled in a Newbury handicap six weeks ago; stable sent out the winner of this race in 2022, 2023 and 2024; work to do at this level.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins in strong handicaps; below best latest; work to do upped to Group 3 company

runners.name: Zoum Zoum
runners.spotlight: Ran poorly on his reappearance at Salisbury (6f, good) five weeks ago; best efforts give him a squeak and he is effective on AW but others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Poor run on seasonal return five weeks ago; enough to prove after that

runners.name: Ferrous
runners.spotlight: Third to Diligent Harry in this race 12 months ago; four creditable runs on AW earlier in the year but others bring more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Third in this race last year up there with his best form; vulnerable for win purposes

races.race_name: Jenningsbet Festival Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation for Northumberland Plate) (GBBPlus)

runners.name: Believitanducan
runners.spotlight: Returned to Flat with short-priced wins (2m, good) at Ascot and Chester, leading over 2f out; firmly in command last time and looks well treated under a 5lb penalty; well beaten in two maidens here in 2023 but a close second in Polytrack handicap in 2025, and that's the sum of his AW record; hitting new heights on turf and a huge player if he's as effective on this surface.
runners.comment: Hitting new heights on turf and a huge player if he's as effective on this surface

runners.name: St Faz
runners.spotlight: Usually races prominently; 4-7 for new yard this year, including two wins on Southwell Tapeta (unraced here); fifth to Shrimp Shady in Chester Plate and ran well again when second at Thirsk last time; each-way chance.
runners.comment: 4-7 for new yard this year; fifth to Shrimp Shady in Chester Plate; each-way chance

runners.name: Fast Fred
runners.spotlight: Disappointing on penultimate start but in good form over 1m4f otherwise this year, with the first of those runs at this track; tried 2m twice last summer and ran well on first occasion despite racing freely; needs to raise his game again, though.
runners.comment: In good form over 1m4f (tried 2m twice last summer) but needs to raise his game again

runners.name: Sax Appeal
runners.spotlight: Very busy and he's had his share of wins, including four (one here) over 2m in 2025; AW has dominated his career; three of his last four turf starts were respectable and he's edging down the weights, so not entirely dismissed.
runners.comment: AW has dominated his career; edging down the weights, so not entirely dismissed

runners.name: Shrimp Shady
runners.spotlight: Pretty consistent in seven AW runs and well backed when leading final 1f to win the 17-runner Chester Plate (2m2f) on last month's return to turf; up 5lb but he's a rising force.
runners.comment: Pretty consistent on AW before well-backed Chester Plate winner; up 5lb but a rising force

runners.name: Arc Zoosve
runners.spotlight: Thriving since the cheekpieces went on in December, winning six of his nine starts in them and that includes his last four races (on Southwell Tapeta four runs back); the big majority of those races were on AW, with a comfortable success at Lingfield last time (has never run at this track); up another 5lb and into a better race but he may well carry on the good work.
runners.comment: Thriving; up another 5lb and into a better race but he may well carry on the good work

runners.name: Clansman
runners.spotlight: Switched to front-running this year, including with Tapeta wins over 1m4f here in March and 2m at Southwell in April when last seen; that's career-best AW form but this race is a much higher grade and should be much harder to boss.
runners.comment: Career-best AW form front-running this year but this race should be much harder to boss
runners.owner: Mrs C M Clarke, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: Hermetic
runners.spotlight: Dual soft-ground winner (1m4f/2m) in France last autumn, from just four races there; picked up for 105,000euros in November and put up a bold show when fourth of 17 at York (1m4f, good) last month; that looked most encouraging but he was tailed off at Goodwood (2m, soft; upset in the stalls) three weeks ago; starkly contrasting messages going into this AW debut.
runners.comment: His two runs for yard were good and bad; upset in stalls last time; makes AW debut

runners.name: Savrola
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who did well throughout five-race campaign last season, most notably when gamely winning this race; 5lb higher today but much more of a worry is that he needs to bounce back from this season's two poor runs on turf.
runners.comment: Won this last year; needs to bounce back from this season's two poor runs on turf

runners.name: Boxing Great
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the ninth attempt in a 2m handicap at Dundalk (2m, Polytrack) in December; this season he's been a never-dangerous eighth to Shrimp Shady at Chester (2m2f) and helped set the pace when sixth of 18 at Fairyhouse (1m6f; just 4-1), not discredited in either but a prominent finish in today's race would be much his best handicap performance.
runners.comment: A prominent finish in today's race would be much his best handicap performance

runners.name: Artisan Dancer
runners.spotlight: Industrious and usually dependable sort at 1m6f-2m2f; turf strike-rate is 1-20 and he was tailed off at Thirsk last time; back to AW and he's 7-34 in this sphere, but he's not shone at this meeting (16th in the Plate, 12th in the Vase) in the last two years.
runners.comment: 7-34 on AW but he's not shone at this meeting in the last two years

runners.name: Haveyoumissedme
runners.spotlight: 8yo who has run 19 times at Newcastle (vastly more than any other track) and had two wins here plus five seconds; second in this race in 2022 and 12th in 2024; his most fruitful trip this year has been 1m2f but he won over 1m6f last November and was a C&D second last August; has a competitive mark too, for a stable in good form.
runners.comment: Second in this race in 2022 and 12th in 2024; competitive mark for a stable in good form

runners.name: Treasure Islands
runners.spotlight: Off the mark when stepping up in trip at Ripon (2m, good; made all) in April; third in a warm Thirsk race on penultimate start, followed by a tidy win at 8-11 in a four-runner race at Catterick this Monday; that gives him a 5lb penalty today and sole AW run was in a novice (okay) but he has more to offer with stamina tests and needs considering.
runners.comment: Lacks AW experience but has more to offer with stamina tests and needs considering

runners.name: Premiere Ligne
runners.spotlight: Very solid record in 2024 on turf but 592 days off before this season; beaten a long way on his return (his only AW race) and not so far next time but he still has major work to do to turn things around.
runners.comment: Very solid record in 2024 on turf but 592 days off before this season; needs a big revival

runners.name: Charging Thunder
runners.spotlight: Not in peak form (hurdles or Flat) from last August onwards but it's not forgotten that he was third in the Plate itself last year off 3lb higher.
runners.comment: Not in peak form (hurdles or Flat) last August onwards; third in 2025 Plate off 3lb higher

runners.name: Brosna Town
runners.spotlight: Stays 1m6f; two Flat wins include a Polytrack handicap at Dundalk in January 2025; went the wrong way last summer/autumn, including over hurdles, and this is his first run since leaving Dermot Weld for £4,000 in April.
runners.comment: Went wrong way in Ireland; has his first run since leaving Dermot Weld for £4,000 in April

runners.name: Day Trader
runners.spotlight: Dual French Flat winner on soft in 2024, who also won a Listowel maiden hurdle last June; he's seemingly sparked only once in six starts since and while he brings a solid AW record, none of that's much beyond 1m4f; his trainer (same as Alphonse Le Grande) always warrants a second look, though.
runners.comment: Second Tony Martin runner; brings a solid AW record but none of that's much beyond 1m4f

runners.name: Chemistry
runners.spotlight: On his third trainer and has rather chequered form but well handicapped judged on best efforts, including his fourth at Southwell (2m, AW) in December off 7lb higher; first and second over hurdles for new yard this spring, with a never-dangerous ninth in the Chester Cup in between; worth a second look.
runners.comment: Well handicapped judged on best efforts and worth a second look
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Alphonse Le Grande
runners.spotlight: Held up; a superb 2024 included wins in the Chester Plate, this race and the Cesarewitch at Newmarket; 2025 campaign was initially promising then frustrating, including with trouble in running, but ended up being disappointing; he's back to his last winning mark, but that's having been always behind at 40-1 in the Chester Cup last time; he will surely spark again at some point but it's hard to know exactly when.
runners.comment: Superb 2024 included a win in this; it's hard to know when he might return to some form

runners.name: Laafy
runners.spotlight: 10yo, no significant impact when very lightly raced in recent years; his only two AW races (neck second in one) were on Polytrack in 2018 and 2019.
runners.comment: 10yo, no significant impact when very lightly raced in recent years

races.race_name: Jenningsbet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Team Player
runners.spotlight: With tongue-tie enlisted he's hit major new heights for new yard this year; started it with wins from off the pace at Newcastle (2m, AW) and Musselburgh (1m6f, soft), both in some style, before his excellent fifth of 15 from out the back in the 2m2f Chester Cup; a bit slow to find his stride that day and cheekpieces now go on; more to give if he takes to them, so a major player.
runners.comment: Two wins (one C&D) before excellent 5th from out the back in Chester Cup; cheekpieces now

runners.name: Bahadur
runners.spotlight: Progressive on AW last autumn, including here, and another improved effort when he won a five-runner race at Goodwood (upped to 2m, good) this May; mid-division at 14-1 in the 2m4f Ascot Stakes 11 days ago, not discredited but he probably needs to resume improvement in another major handicap today.
runners.comment: Not discredited at Royal Ascot; probably needs to resume progress in another big handicap
runners.owner: J William Rowley & S P C Woods

runners.name: Ride The Thunder
runners.spotlight: Won 1m2f maiden last June; on the premises in all his four handicaps, coming from off the pace when second at Haydock (1m6f) on final 3yo start and when third at Hamilton (1m5f; gelded and hooded) on reappearance six weeks ago; relatively lightly raced, so could have further improvement in him and 2m is plausible for this AW debut.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; could have further improvement in him and 2m is plausible for this AW debut

runners.name: Tribal Star
runners.spotlight: Brought over from Ireland for five British races and ran well every time, including when fourth in this contest last year and third in the Chester Plate last month; he didn't win any of them, though, and has taken just one Irish handicap; plenty seemingly needs to go right if he's to win this but he has a place chance despite wide draw.
runners.comment: 4th in this last year and 3rd in Chester Plate last month; place chance despite wide draw

runners.name: Circus Of Rome
runners.spotlight: Third win last term was clearcut over 1m4f here in August and he ran creditably over 1m6f on turf next time; it's not that hard to forgive this season, given the 1m2f/1m, although he has raced a bit freely; looked the type to make progress this year and this is an eye-catching rethink on trip.
runners.comment: Stays 1m6f but unraced at 2m; the type to make progress this year after his 1m2f/1m runs

runners.name: Blazeon Five
runners.spotlight: Ended last year in storming form with a 1m6f/2m hat-trick, the final leg on Southwell Tapeta; not disgraced when never dangerous here in January and made a satisfactory return to turf (second at Newbury) in April, but this 8yo needs to hit another new high.
runners.comment: Made a satisfactory return in April but this 8yo needs to hit another new high

runners.name: Synergism
runners.spotlight: Last season included four wins and a good C&D second to Sax Appeal; a hard-working winner at evens in four-runner race at Lingfield (1m6f, good) last month, which he needs to improve on if he's to defy a 5lb penalty but that was a solid start to his 4yo campaign and he could still have potential.
runners.comment: Needs to improve under 5lb penalty but his May win was a solid start to this 4yo campaign

runners.name: Spirit Mixer
runners.spotlight: Beat Dancing In Paris by a neck to win this race last year off 6lb lower; also won at Chester in September and two of his three starts this term have been creditable, so he's in the each-way picture.
runners.comment: Won this race last year off 6lb lower; two of three starts this term have been creditable

runners.name: Dancing In Paris
runners.spotlight: He hasn't won since August 2024 but ran some fine races last season, including when beaten a neck by Spirit Mixer in this event; 4lb higher today and made a muted reappearance at Epsom three weeks ago but that was at 40-1 over 1m4f on soft; should be more interesting again with that under his belt.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck in this last year and is 4lb higher today; has one 2026 run under his belt

runners.name: Asgard's Captain
runners.spotlight: Three wins at this track but he came 13th in this race last year; that was his only appearance over this long a trip and he was also below form when he had a crack at 1m6f, so he's unproven beyond 1m4f, over which he's contested his last nine starts; stamina is the big doubt.
runners.comment: Three wins at this track but he came 13th in this race last year; stamina is the big doubt

runners.name: Kirchner
runners.spotlight: 1m win on the first of five runs in France; never dangerous at 50-1 and 25-1 with cheekpieces over 1m4f when he was finally seen in Britain for two handicaps last month but he did make some late headway, particularly when fifth of 13 on Kempton Polytrack last time; marked overall improvement is needed but he's a lightly raced, unexposed 4yo stepping up in trip.
runners.comment: Overall improvement is needed but he's a lightly raced, unexposed 4yo stepping up in trip

runners.name: Moon Over Miami
runners.spotlight: Third turf win was a narrow one off this mark at Newbury (2m, good; rallying) in April on his final start last year; ran creditably once in 2025 on Polytrack and it's not hard to make excuses for his three turf runs this year, including the Chester Cup, but a revival is needed and such a wide draw probably makes that harder.
runners.comment: It's not hard to make excuses for his three turf runs this year but a revival is needed

runners.name: Aqwaam
runners.spotlight: Three AW wins include his only appearance at this track, over C&D in 2023; sole win since was in March 2024; excellent third off this mark in the Cesarewitch at Newmarket that October but unraced since until well held at 66-1 over 1m4f one month ago; whether that run is enough to have brought him back to peak form is hard to say with confidence but he shaped okay.
runners.comment: Third in the Cesarewitch at Newmarket in 2024; shaped okay last month on first run since

runners.name: Elysian Flame
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in 2018 in his only AW race; peak Flat form came with big-race placings over 2m4f in summer 2021; there's been a four-year absence since and his 2m win at Beverley (2lb higher today) this April showed that stacks of his ability remains, form which was backed up at Thirsk on latest start but this contest demands more; cheekpieces (absent this term) return.
runners.comment: Stacks of his ability remains but this demands more; cheekpieces (off this term) return
runners.owner: J Blackburn & Mrs C A Coward

runners.name: Gentleman Joe
runners.spotlight: Has Polytrack form, most of it in his Irish days with Henry De Bromhead; well treated on some 2025 form but he's not shown enough for new yard this season (tailed off over C&D first occasion); cheekpieces are recruited.
runners.comment: Well treated on some 2025 form but he's not shown enough for new yard this season

runners.name: Align The Stars
runners.spotlight: Broke his losing run in clearcut style when he reappeared at Kempton (2m, AW) in March with restored cheekpieces, which have been kept since; good second in valuable race at Newmarket (1m6f) but he needs to bounce back from a disappointing show at Goodwood five weeks ago.
runners.comment: Needs to bounce back from a modest show five weeks ago; had turned a corner before that

runners.name: Prydwen
runners.spotlight: Prolific 8yo who gained his 11th success when taking 1m4f Ripon handicap in April; also effective at 2m this term, including C&D, but he's edged back up the weights; place player but he may be vulnerable for win purposes.
runners.comment: Effective over C&D; place player but he may be vulnerable for win purposes

runners.name: Saint Etienne
runners.spotlight: Four wins (three AW) over about 1m2f from 22 races in France; 50-1 fourth at York (1m4f) in May was an encouraging third run for Brian Ellison, after which he was never dangerous at 25-1 off this reduced mark in the 1m2f Zetland Gold Cup at Redcar; tongue tied every time except for those last two starts and he is again today; chief conundrum, though, has to be with this first attempt beyond 1m4f.
runners.comment: AW winner in France; chief conundrum has to be this first attempt beyond 1m4f
runners.owner: The Cat And The Mag Partnership

runners.name: Zanndabad
runners.spotlight: Two novice hurdles last autumn seem a pretty meagre return for respected yard but he was an unlucky, strong-finishing third in the 2024 Chester Cup; glimpses of that Flat ability on occasions since, but he made inroads far too late from out the back in this last year (11th at 13-2) and it was a similar story in last month's Chester Cup (8th at 11-1); something needs to change and such a wide draw probably means he tries to come from the back again.
runners.comment: Made inroads far too late from out the back in this last year and last month's Chester Cup

runners.name: Tashkhan
runners.spotlight: Off since a mixed hurdles campaign ended in March; formerly very smart on Flat but he's not scored in this sphere since 2023, had a disappointing 2025 and all wins have been on soft or heavy ground; tailed off in this race in 2024.
runners.comment: All wins have been on soft or heavy ground; tailed off in this race in 2024

races.race_name: Jenningsbet Over 200 Shops Nationwide Handicap

runners.name: Power Fizz
runners.spotlight: Progressive at 7f in 2025 and he returned from ten months off to run out an easy winner of a Class 3 handicap over C&D (2-2 at Newcastle) in March; up 7lb but foolish to think he's not up to the task; stable won this in 2024.
runners.comment: 2-2 at Newcastle, impressing over C&D in March; hasn't finished progressing just yet

runners.name: Cool Hoof Luke
runners.spotlight: 2024 Gimcrack winner; good third at Lingfield on his belated reappearance before winning at Wolverhampton (7f, Listed; fine ride) in March; below par on turf twice since but likely to make a bold bid back on AW.
runners.comment: Two good AW runs this year; struggled on turf last two runs; this should be more suitable

runners.name: Tuco Salamanca
runners.spotlight: Three runs at Newcastle, winning two of them and desperately unlucky in the 3yo handicap last Good Friday on the other; looked set for another productive season when scoring at Kempton in March and a subsequent York defeat shouldn't be used as conclusive evidence the handicapper has caught up; leading claims back on AW.
runners.comment: Good course record and impressed on seasonal return; latest turf run respectable; solid

runners.name: Al Najashi
runners.spotlight: Form took off once handicapping in a tongue tie and his impressive C&D win on Good Friday, his fifth on the bounce, was off 32lb higher than the first; progress ground to a halt on turf last month but not out of the question the return to AW will perk him back up.
runners.comment: Won first five handicaps, notably over C&D on Good Friday; not so good on turf last month

runners.name: Rapper's Delight
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of his 3yo starts, getting off the mark in a 5f novice at Southwell last September; ran creditably in Dubai earlier in the year without looking ahead of his mark; returns to action in a tough race and the new visor needs to have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Beaten in three handicaps in Dubai earlier in the year; now visored; mark looks tough

runners.name: Fahrenheit Seven
runners.spotlight: Began the year with a pair of impressive 5f handicap wins on AW (here and at Southwell) and he didn't have ideal track position when finishing in midfield in a Naas Listed event in April; too bad to be true at York last time; equally effective at 5f and 6f; remains of interest.
runners.comment: Two fine AW wins (5f) this year; too bad to be true at York latest; solid contender

runners.name: Caburn
runners.spotlight: Useful as 2yo sprinter; successful when sights lowered at Yarmouth last September and his third in a 6f Listed race at Doncaster (good to soft) in March was his best form; hard to call him well handicapped now though.
runners.comment: Third at Doncaster (6f; Listed) in March; flopped on fast ground latest; high in weights

runners.name: Pocklington
runners.spotlight: Heavily backed when winning a 6f handicap at Southwell on New Year's Day; ran well over C&D a fortnight later but short of his best twice since, including after wind surgery when eighth of 22 at York last month; returning to AW can help and seems highly likely to run his race.
runners.comment: Strong AW form this winter; below par on turf latest but this should be more his thing

runners.name: Strike Red
runners.spotlight: Twice a C&D winner; ended losing run with a hard-fought success at Leicester in April and he ran creditably at Epsom on Derby day; minor money perhaps his best hope.
runners.comment: Conditions no problem and still feasibly weighted; few secrets after 57 runs though

runners.name: Topwarrior
runners.spotlight: The addition of blinkers saw him take a good step forward when comfortably seeing off 17 rivals at Newbury (6f, soft) last October; absent until Derby day three weeks ago where he was never a threat; the blinkers now return and dangerous to underestimate him.
runners.comment: Impressed at Newbury last autumn when blinkered first time; headgear back on today; chance

runners.name: Annaf
runners.spotlight: Smart performer on his day; finished second in the Group 3 on this card 12 months ago and ended 2025 with an Ascot win at that level; it has been a mixed back so far in 2026 but he is edging down the weights and has too much ability to discount; accessories tried last month are now dispensed with.
runners.comment: Second in Group 3 on this card last year; mixed bag in 2026 but dropping down the weights

runners.name: Fivethousandtoone
runners.spotlight: Faced with his optimum conditions but he is 3lb higher than for his win in this race last summer; each-way claims once again.
runners.comment: These are his conditions and won this race last year off 3lb lower; each-way shout again

runners.name: Knebworth
runners.spotlight: Reliable operator on AW and after wins at Chelmsford and Wolverhampton he found only Tuco Salamanca too strong in his hat-trick bid in March; better off with that rival today but not sure he's quite so progressive.
runners.comment: Chased home Tuco Salamanca at Kempton in March; not so progressive as that rival

runners.name: The Caltonian
runners.spotlight: Swept through from off the pace to win a good C&D handicap in January; well beaten in two runs since and surprising if there weren't a few stronger in this field.
runners.comment: C&D win in January has been followed by two lesser efforts; others look safer
runners.owner: John Murphy & Miss L A Perratt

races.race_name: Pertemps Network EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Knock Three Times
runners.spotlight: Both runs over 6f on good ground; her debut second hasn't really worked out and although it appeared a backwards step when beaten over 5l at Goodwood five weeks ago, the winner ran a fine race at Royal Ascot; this stiff 5f should be fine and she is one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two 6f turf events, latterly behind a smart filly; contender dropped to 5f

runners.name: Papercut
runners.spotlight: First foal of a multiple 6f winner (including the Group 3 Chipchase Stakes run on this card five years ago), herself from a good family; looks the part on paper and he's the most interesting of the newcomers.
runners.comment: Dam a Group 3 winner at this track; likely type on paper; betting revealing

runners.name: Bacha
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros yearling, £80,000 breeze-up 2yo; third foal; dam placed 5f/5.7f (RPR 69), half-sister to winners Spiritual Star (useful 7f-8.6f including 2yo) and Lady Princess (6f 2yo Listed); stable's newcomers often better for a run but they have had a newcomer win on AW this season.
runners.comment: £80,000 breeze-up 2yo; dam maiden half-sister to couple of useful winners; market to guide

runners.name: Hell Of A Spin
runners.spotlight: 65,000euros yearling, 75,000euros breeze-up 2yo; fourth foal; half-brother to 6f winner Smokey Bear (including 2yo/AW/Hong Kong; RPR 102); dam French 12.5f AW winner (70), half-sister to US 1m2f Grade 1 winner Shamdinan and Australian 1m4f Group 2 winner Shahwardi; by a speed influence but the bottom line of his pedigree raises slight doubts over 5f.
runners.comment: 75,000euros breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to useful 6f winner Smokey Bear (RPR 102); 5f a ?

runners.name: Taj Mahal Palace
runners.spotlight: 22,500gns yearling, £30,000 breeze-up 2yo (private sale); sixth foal; half-brother to winners Knebworth (5f-6f including AW 2yo; RPR 98) and Golden Rainbow (5f including 2yo/AW; 77); dam unraced half-sister to prolific 5f winner Archimedes, out of 6f Listed winner; stable's only 2yo winner this season came in a seller.
runners.comment: £30,000 breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to two AW winners; fair standard to aim at

runners.name: Barnaby Rudge
runners.spotlight: It wasn't the strongest Nottingham (5f, good to soft) novice that he won on his debut 16 days ago but he did it easily and that despite greenness; always encouraging when one from the yard wins first time out; strong claims despite a 6lb penalty.
runners.comment: Easy win in a Nottingham novice 16 days ago; strong claims under a 6lb penalty

runners.name: Flashing Star
runners.spotlight: Too green to do himself justice on his debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft) but he was staying on well when the penny dropped and would have snatched third in a couple more strides; although that was a lowly Band D maiden, he was notably strong in the market and should be capable of better; sire's progeny have a much better record on AW than turf so cause for optimism there too; this does look warmer though.
runners.comment: Too green when favourite on last month's Beverley debut; sire's progeny do better on AW
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing, J Craggs & Partner

races.race_name: Pertemps Network Educational Handicap

runners.name: It's Only Fun
runners.spotlight: Was having just his third go on AW when taking a Southwell handicap (1m3f, Tapeta) last month and he backed it up with slightly unlucky second at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) three weeks ago; 1lb nudge looks no bother at all and he's near the top of the list.
runners.comment: Third go on AW when scoring last month and backed it up on turf next time; key player

runners.name: Golspie
runners.spotlight: AW regular who scored here (1m2f) in December and was a solid second over C&D in March; blew the start but ran respectably when fourth on turf at Chester a fortnight ago and he's handicapped to be competitive again.
runners.comment: Scored here (1m2f) in December; solid C&D second in March; latest fine and he's in the mix

runners.name: Billy Bathgate
runners.spotlight: Four wins last season included two here at 2m; never figured in this race last term but he's been in the frame all nine starts this year and was unlucky when fifth at this track in December on his last go at 1m4f; each-way prospects.
runners.comment: Unlucky fifth over C&D in December; in the frame all nine starts this year; each-way shout

runners.name: Elemental Eye
runners.spotlight: 0-5 on AW and he was never a threat over C&D in March; remote third over 2m at Musselburgh (good) three weeks ago and he now drops back in trip with added blinkers.
runners.comment: 0-5 on AW; remote third over 2m on turf three weeks ago; blinkers given a go

runners.name: Romantic Spirit
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner at up to 1m3f but she's disappointed in both starts this year; has dipped below her last winning mark, however, so can't be confidently ruled out tackling this trip for the first time.
runners.comment: Four-time AW winner at up to 1m3f but disappointing in both starts this year; mark fine

runners.name: Jujubella
runners.spotlight: Not at the top of her game this season but she's dropped down the weights and took a step back in the right direction when fifth at Hamilton (1m5f, good) three weeks ago; creditable third of four at Hamilton on Thursday; three wins here include one at this trip and she warrants a good look.
runners.comment: Three wins here include one at this trip; she warrants a good look

runners.name: Sure And Stedfast
runners.spotlight: Two-time turf winner (1m1f/1m2f) who arrives in good nick after two solid placed efforts at Hamilton (1m3f/1m5f); hasn't shone in two starts here but otherwise he's more solid than most.
runners.comment: Hasn't shone in two starts here but he arrives in good nick and is more solid than most

runners.name: Big Win
runners.spotlight: Fairly lightly raced 4yo who left Harry Charlton after an underwhelming effort over C&D in October; swiftly in the groove for this yard and he followed encouraging second at Bath (1m6f, good to firm) in May by landing breakthrough success at Chepstow (1m4f, soft) three weeks ago; acts on Tapeta and he's firmly considered up 5lb.
runners.comment: Landed breakthrough success at Chepstow (1m4f, soft) three weeks ago; firmly considered

runners.name: Arth's Gold
runners.spotlight: Capable of the odd howler but he opened his account in Southwell handicap (1m4f, Tapeta) in April and similar form when third in an amateur riders' race back there three weeks later; wasn't at his best in another amateur riders' race last time but he's recovered from blips before.
runners.comment: Opened account (1m4f, Tapeta) in April; not so good latest but he's got over blips before

runners.name: Skinny Malinka
runners.spotlight: Pulled up lame over hurdles on his first go for this yard and he received a 62l humbling back on the Flat at Carlisle (1m1f, soft) 19 days ago; good form case to be made on previous efforts for Ed Bethell but can't be considered until he starts showing something.
runners.comment: Received a 62l humbling back on the Flat 19 days ago; hard to fancy

runners.name: Free World
runners.spotlight: Back from a 488-day absence when suffering a heavy defeat on his British/stable debut in May; second go for this yard was better but he still faded to finish last a fortnight ago; will probably come good for respected stable at some point and the market will help to decipher if it's today.
runners.comment: Better effort on second go for yard but still finished last; needs a cursory market check

runners.name: Cavalry Call
runners.spotlight: 1m turf and 1m2f AW winner for the Crisfords but he never took off for Mike Smith and finished last on his stable debut six weeks ago; impossible to recommend without market support.
runners.comment: Finished last on stable debut six weeks ago; can't recommend without market support

runners.name: Character Testing
runners.spotlight: 1m AW winner for Tom Clover who has been mostly hurdling for this yard; went close in January and again in Cartmel seller on penultimate start; didn't shape with much promise in two turf handicaps (1m/1m2f) in between but his Flat mark has come down quite sharply.
runners.comment: Has been hurdling; will need to better two Flat runs in April but he's on a reduced mark

runners.name: Natzor
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m2f course winner for David O'Meara in 2024; close 100-1 fourth here (again 1m2f) in March was a standout for this yard but otherwise it's been a struggle; has quite a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Close 100-1 fourth here (1m2f) in March was a standout for this yard; otherwise struggled

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Furturra
runners.spotlight: Proving a tough sort; performing well in sellers, including over C&D, prior to easily justifying favouritism in novice contest at Redcar (6f, good) last Saturday; likely to remain competitive even with 4lb penalty.
runners.comment: Proving a tough sort; performing well in sellers prior to easy win in Redcar novice race

runners.name: Sirocco Sands
runners.spotlight: Irish colt who has made the frame in both starts, latest at the Curragh (6f, good to yielding) where he fared best of the near-side runners; first two in that race subsequently dipped their toes into Royal Ascot waters; solid claims.
runners.comment: Irish colt who has made the frame in both starts, latest at the Curragh; solid claims

runners.name: Mezzo Forte
runners.spotlight: Newcomer by Wootton Bassett and first foal of a 6f/7f 2yo winner (including Group 3; RPR 99); attractively bred and represents major stable; interesting contender, especially if the betting speaks favourably.
runners.comment: Newcomer by Wootton Bassett and first foal of a 2yo Group 3 winner; interesting contender

runners.name: Sue's Last Chance
runners.spotlight: Has shown promise in a couple of C&D contests on good ground, despite being a 22-1 shot both times; finished fifth to a smart prospect latest, taking a step forward on the figures; in the mix.
runners.comment: Has shown promise in a couple of C&D contests, latest behind a smart prospect; in the mix

runners.name: Casino Star
runners.spotlight: Showed signs of inexperience on his way to finishing second of five in Hamilton maiden (6f, good to soft) won by colt who franked the form by subsequently running very well in the Coventry; warrants respect with progress on the cards.
runners.comment: Second at Hamilton behind a colt who subsequently ran very well in the Coventry; respected

runners.name: Cagney
runners.spotlight: 28,000euros foal, £65,000 yearling; Earthlight half-brother to four winners, notably Amica Nostra (French 1m2f including Listed; RPR 102); dam 7f/1m scorer (81), out of 6f 2yo Listed winner; heed the market signals.
runners.comment: £65,000 yearling; Earthlight half-brother to four winners; heed the market signals

runners.name: Washington Royale
runners.spotlight: Washington DC half-brother to seven winners, mostly for his connections and broadly improving with experience; very likely to need this first outing.
runners.comment: Half-brother to several winners for his connections but is likely to need this first run

runners.name: Yorkshire Dream
runners.spotlight: Has a poor chance on his 5f form and is one of a few runners who'll find nurseries a better fit.
runners.comment: Has a poor chance on his 5f form

runners.name: Rbflying
runners.spotlight: Holds weak claims on his 5f efforts and should find nurseries more suitable soon.
runners.comment: Holds weak claims on his 5f efforts

runners.name: Spirit Of Mary
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who has RPRs in the 40s, latest effort at claiming level; stiff task.
runners.comment: Has RPRs in the 40s; stiff task

runners.name: Aodhan
runners.spotlight: Only eighth of nine in the Redcar event won by Furturra; needs to leave that debut effort well behind.
runners.comment: Only eighth of nine in the Redcar event won by Furturra

runners.name: Bigalo
runners.spotlight: Has plenty to find on his two efforts and may find life easier in nurseries in the near future.
runners.comment: Has plenty to find on his two efforts

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Mile Handicap

runners.name: Zennor Storm
runners.spotlight: Two from two as a 2yo, both starts on Kempton AW; has finished last in a couple of turf handicaps this season but a subsequent gelding operation may prompt a turnaround and he represents a major trainer whose last two York runners, both in competitive races two Saturdays ago, were successful; by no means written off.
runners.comment: Gelding operation may prompt a turnaround; trainer's last two York runners were successful

runners.name: Andesite
runners.spotlight: Rather quirky sort who has become a slow-starter and is 0-12 since a successful debut at the 2024 York Dante meeting but is threatening to double his tally; met traffic issues on his way to a very notable effort at this venue (7f, good) most recently, beating all bar a handicap snip who remains unbeaten and looks a Group performer in the making; shaped as if this second attempt over 1m is well worth exploring; highly respected.
runners.comment: Notable effort at York last time, beating all bar a handicap snip who remains unbeaten

runners.name: Blue To Blue
runners.spotlight: Scored three times last term for previous yard; below par in latest outing but ran well at Haydock (1m) earlier in May; this 3yo looks a possible danger off a favourable weight, provided he bounces back.
runners.comment: Ran well on penultimate start; this 3yo looks a possible danger off a favourable weight

runners.name: Delinquent
runners.spotlight: In-form 3yo who is 2-6 since handicapping; fitted with cheekpieces in last two starts, winning at Hamilton then creditable fifth in notable event at Carlisle, both over 1m; likely player off bottom weight in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: In-form 3yo who is 2-6 since handicapping; ran creditably in notable event last time

runners.name: Golden Strike
runners.spotlight: Record of 4-15 overall and 2-5 since wearing visor; campaigned exclusively over shorter but the manner of his half-length success in big field at York (7f, good) last time suggests this new trip is worth exploring; enters calculations in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Record of 2-5 since wearing visor; prevailed in big field over 7f at York most recently

runners.name: Jimmy Speaking
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably at Newmarket when bidding for a hat-trick and was likely a little out of his depth in Class 2 at Epsom since; however, battling with the assessor as he remains on career-high mark.
runners.comment: Drops back in class but he's battling with the assessor (remains on career-high mark)

runners.name: Frankies Dream
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate is 6-26; soon in a consistent vein of form this term and was beaten only half a length by the market leader in York event (1m1f, good) most recently, taking record at this venue to 512; solid.
runners.comment: Soon in a consistent vein of form this term; latest effort took his York record to 512

runners.name: Tailgunner Joe
runners.spotlight: Has failed to convince over 1m2f since returning to home soil; gelded prior to latest start; 1m winner during his campaign at Meydan and the return to this distance looks the angle with him.
runners.comment: 1m winner during his campaign at Meydan; return to this distance is the angle with him

runners.name: Wild Nature
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-3 on Kempton AW; 0-8 on turf but has been placed in half of those runs, most recently at Musselburgh on seasonal debut; could go well with Oisin Murphy up for first time.
runners.comment: 0-8 on turf but has been placed in half of those runs; Oisin Murphy booked; could go well

runners.name: Cadarn
runners.spotlight: Record at Redcar comprises his two wins for previous yard and best effort for new stable; York form comprises poor efforts in this race last year and in latest outing; something to prove.
runners.comment: Seems particularly well suited by Redcar; his York form is far from convincing

runners.name: Wreck It Ryley
runners.spotlight: Raced exclusively at shorter, gaining most wins over 6f; looks likely to be outstayed and outclassed upped in trip/grade.
runners.comment: Looks likely to be outstayed and outclassed upped in trip/grade

runners.name: Wahdan
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-5 in France then 0-7 in Ireland; midfield in York handicap (1m2f) on stable/seasonal debut when the 150-1 rank outsider; perhaps best watched unless the market signals go the other way.
runners.comment: Midfield in York handicap on stable/seasonal debut when the 150-1 rank outsider

runners.name: Eligible
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has a better strike-rate on AW than on turf and hasn't prevailed in this sphere since York success three years ago; midfield when the 125-1 rank outsider back here last time.
runners.comment: Veteran who has a better strike-rate on AW than on turf

runners.name: Sujet
runners.spotlight: Ran encouragingly in first couple of starts for new stable but has posted two duck eggs kept to 7f since, latest in competitive field at York; doesn't look as if he's about to add to his Irish wins.
runners.comment: Ran encouragingly in first two starts for new yard; form has dipped since

runners.name: Darkness
runners.spotlight: Has won over 1m but raced mainly at 7f; not solid on 2026 form and he came up short when a 50-1 shot in big field at York most recently; opposed in another competitive race, back up in trip.
runners.comment: Raced mainly at 7f; not solid on 2026 form; came up short at York last time

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dash Handicap

runners.name: Shes Got A Brother
runners.spotlight: Commenced her career with two April wins on Southwell AW; good efforts in Epsom/York handicaps (won by Fortification and Naana's Shadow) and Ayr Listed race since; raised 5lb for latest effort but again looks likely to go well; has progressive RPRs.
runners.comment: Progressive form includes good efforts in races won by Fortification and Naana's Shadow

runners.name: Allsortz
runners.spotlight: Irish sprinter who is building up a solid record; successful at Cork (soft) and good third at the Curragh (good to yielding) since dropped from 6f to 5f; may be capable of better still, particularly over this distance; firmly in the mix.
runners.comment: Irish sprinter who is building up a solid record, latterly dropped from 6f to 5f

runners.name: Alfa Duplicate
runners.spotlight: Held on gamely at Beverley to add to her AW wins; respectable sixth to Naana's Shadow at Epsom since, albeit without showing any further progress; has perhaps reached her ceiling.
runners.comment: Respectable sixth to Naana's Shadow at Epsom, albeit showing no further progress

runners.name: Naana's Shadow
runners.spotlight: Improving filly who justified favouritism under Oisin Murphy in the 3yo Dash at Epsom most recently, taking overall strike-rate to 3-8 and record in handicaps to 2-4; good third behind Fortification and Stargazed over C&D the time before; strong contender.
runners.comment: Improving filly who landed the 3yo Dash at Epsom most recently; strong contender

runners.name: Schrodinger's Cat
runners.spotlight: Has a largely solid record and may remain competitive despite taking an 8lb hike for an emphatic success, having bossed a small field, at Redcar (5f, good) last Saturday; good third of 13 over C&D the time before and doesn't necessarily need to dominate.
runners.comment: Raised 8lb for Redcar win but may remain competitive; has a largely solid record

runners.name: Our Cody
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-10 but she's fairly useful and has performed well in 5f contests (handicap/Listed) at Sandown this term; perhaps best not to take latest effort literally but in going back up just 1lb the assessor hasn't overreacted; again has frame possibilities.
runners.comment: Has performed well in two races (handicap/Listed) at Sandown this season

runners.name: Fortification
runners.spotlight: Has maintained his consistency by posting form figures of 143, all in sprints at York, since joining current stable; beat Stargazed by half a length on the first occasion and ran well, despite a tricky draw the way things panned out, in notable handicap most recently; very solid claims.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 143, all in York sprints, since joining new yard; very solid claims

runners.name: Rotokura Belle
runners.spotlight: In-form filly who prevailed by a neck in Class 4 contest over C&D (good) last time; however, needs further improvement to defy her new mark and follow up in this significantly better race.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D last time but needs further improvement to follow up in this grade

runners.name: Front Line Fury
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly over 6f but gives the impression this return to 5f may prove beneficial and thus looks an interesting contender off a mark 7lb lower than his peak one; proved resurgent behind a stronger finisher at Kempton (AW) last time.
runners.comment: Interesting contender as he gives the impression this return to 5f is worth exploring

runners.name: Mighty Magnus
runners.spotlight: Has gained both wins in Scotland, including at Musselburgh (5f, good; did it readily) latest start; generally consistent sort but will need further progress to defy a 5lb rise and follow up in this field.
runners.comment: Has gained both wins in Scotland, including latest start; this is a stiffer task

runners.name: Hanney Girl
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in the York/Epsom races won by Fortification and Naana's Shadow but is fairly exposed at this point, making her a similar type to stablemate Go Vince Go.
runners.comment: Similar type to stablemate Go Vince Go, being fairly exposed at this point

runners.name: Stargazed
runners.spotlight: Has very encouraging form switched to handicaps this term, namely a solid second (split Fortification and Naana's Shadow) over C&D then respectable 12th of 27 when pitched into deeper waters at Royal Ascot; may still have further improvement in her; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Solid second over C&D on reappearance, then ran respectably at Royal Ascot; likely player

runners.name: Go Vince Go
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D last October but is reasonably exposed now and faces a stiffer assignment in this field; came up well short in a notable 6f handicap back here last time.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D last October but is reasonably exposed now and this is harder

runners.name: Manatee Mehmas
runners.spotlight: Looked above-average in commencing his career with an emphatic win at Doncaster and creditable fourth in C&D Listed event, both this spring; didn't fare so well at Sandown last time but may still have progress in him; bids to defy top weight on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Thrice-raced gelding who may still have progress in him; has a piece of C&D Listed form

runners.name: Boston Dan
runners.spotlight: Good second in sales and Listed races over 6f at York during his 2yo campaign; not in anything like the same form this term, including here, and still needs to prove that he's trained on; record is only 1-12.
runners.comment: Ran well twice at York last term but still needs to prove that he's trained on

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Criterion Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Never So Brave
runners.spotlight: Smart performer; completed a notable hat-trick (on good to firm ground) last summer, culminating in Group 1 honours over C&D; avoids a penalty for that success and holds a leading chance assuming he's back in similar form; best to forgive Epsom Group 3 effort last time (unsuited by the way the race unfolded).
runners.comment: Avoids a penalty for his Group 1 win over C&D last August; best to forgive latest effort

runners.name: Saber Strike
runners.spotlight: Ran below expectations when a warm order for the Jersey Stakes at Royal Ascot last Saturday but was drawn a bit higher than ideal, the way the race panned out, and finishing sixth of 16 wasn't a total disaster; 2-2 previously, including 7f Listed win at Newmarket, and can be treated as retaining potential; respected in receipt of 9lb from his elders.
runners.comment: Not ideally drawn when beaten favourite in the Jersey; 2-2 previously; retains potential

runners.name: Qirat
runners.spotlight: Not totally solid on his form since 150-1 success when pacemaker in last year's Sussex Stakes (1m, good); however, looks interesting back down in trip, with his 7f record reading 1211225 (ran creditably behind Never So Brave in C&D Group 1 the final time); fitting of tongue-tie may also contribute to a resurgence.
runners.comment: 150-1 Sussex Stakes winner; interesting back down in trip, with 7f record reading 1211225

runners.name: Royal Velvet
runners.spotlight: Always behind in the boiling cauldron of a Royal Ascot handicap last week; progressive otherwise and still has good stats, being 9-22 overall, 2-3 this term and 6-11 over 7f (1-1 over C&D); however, has to overcome a 3lb penalty for the Group 3 win achieved against her own sex in May.
runners.comment: Has to overcome a 3lb penalty for the Group 3 win achieved against her own sex in May

runners.name: Balmacara
runners.spotlight: Useful but is 0-12 since completing a Doncaster novice double in spring 2024, essentially proving quite difficult to place; hasn't strictly improved for a switch to hold-up tactics this term (seemingly being taught to settle better); unlikely to regain the winning thread on these terms.
runners.comment: 0-12 since novice double in spring 2024; unlikely to end the drought on these terms

runners.name: Chicago Critic
runners.spotlight: Comes from off the pace; has made the frame several times at Group level since his Listed win 13 months ago; not disgraced at Epsom most recently, especially considering the race panned out unfavourably; however, chance again partly depends on whether the cards drop right.
runners.comment: Comes from off the pace; chance again partly depends on whether the cards drop right

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Rock N Roll Pinkie
runners.spotlight: Progressive six-time winner whose last two successes came over 2m here (good to soft/good) in July and September; outclassed in a course Group 3 last month but had previously been running respectably and this return to handicaps will see her in much better light; considered.
runners.comment: Progressive six-time winner; will be seen in much better light back in a h'cap; considered

runners.name: Who's Lope
runners.spotlight: Built on promising return over 1m2f when seeing out 1m4f strongly to land three-runner novice at Beverley four weeks ago; almost certainly capable of better, particularly over this trip, and he's a very interesting newcomer to handicaps.
runners.comment: Saw out 1m4f strongly to win novice four weeks ago; up in trip for h'cap debut; key player

runners.name: Draupnir
runners.spotlight: 7f winner at Thirsk (soft) in September and he was back in that sort of form when close third at Hamilton (1m1f, good) three weeks ago; sire and damsire both influences for stamina but this marked step up in trip is still a little surprising.
runners.comment: 7f winner who was a close third (1m1f) latest; this is a marked step up in trip

runners.name: Imperial Sovereign
runners.spotlight: No wins since an odds-on debut success for Karl Burke back in 2023 but he's run with credit on plenty of occasions; that wasn't the case at Chester last time, however, and on balance he's opposable despite this career-low mark.
runners.comment: Yet to add to debut success in 2023; below par latest; opposable despite career-low mark

runners.name: Talismans Time
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who won a Hamilton maiden (1m3f) and handicap (1m5f) on his first two starts of last year; finished tailed off in the Melrose over C&D next time, however, and he weakened quickly before being dismounted at Musselburgh on his comeback; difficult to suggest after that.
runners.comment: Finished tailed off in C&D Melrose and weakened quickly before dismounted on his comeback

runners.name: Secret Force
runners.spotlight: Good start for this yard when in the frame at Hamilton (1m3f) and here (1m4f) and he knuckled down well to score over 2m2f at Pontefract (also good) on Sunday; that was the first time he'd worn this headgear combination and he's respected under a 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: Knuckled down well to score over 2m2f in this headgear on Sunday; respected under penalty

runners.name: My Ballyquinn
runners.spotlight: Progressive 3yo who landed three-runner event on his handicap debut at Hamilton (1m3f) in May and went very close to defying a 4lb rise in another small field at Doncaster one week ago; up another 4lb but there's plenty of stamina in the family and this trip can unlock more.
runners.comment: Progressive 3yo; won h'cap debut and went very close next time; this trip can unlock more

runners.name: Gibside
runners.spotlight: Handles most surfaces and his eight wins include two here, latterly over 2m in 2024; last scored at Catterick (1m4f) off 3lb higher in October and after some lesser efforts this season he was much more competitive when third back there (1m6f) a fortnight ago; contender to step forward again and he's considered.
runners.comment: 8 wins include 2 here; back in some form when third last time and could build on that

runners.name: Terrorise
runners.spotlight: Won here (2m, good to firm) in July but an 8lb rise signalled a reversal in his fortunes and he's beaten one horse in both starts this term; nothing wrong with this mark if the visor prompts a turnaround, though.
runners.comment: Beat one horse in both starts this term; nothing wrong with this mark if the visor works

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Sprint Handicap

runners.name: Hallo Spaceboy
runners.spotlight: Consistent in first four starts, including an AW 5f win in November, but he took it up a notch when a neck second of 19 at Ascot (6f, good) seven weeks ago; raised 5lb but he's a leading contender.
runners.comment: Good second at Ascot (6f, good) seven weeks ago; raised 5lb but he's a leading contender

runners.name: Explosive Finnish
runners.spotlight: He was stuck in the placings when running well this spring and again couldn't quite put it to bed when runner-up at Chester (6f, soft; acts on quicker) a fortnight ago; mark is creeping up but it would be no surprise to see him in the thick of it once more.
runners.comment: Mark is creeping up but it deserves to and no surprise to see him in the thick of it again

runners.name: Bye Law
runners.spotlight: Won Ayr maiden and Redcar novice last summer; 0-8 in handicaps but he's started to knock on the door from this reduced mark with a pair of third-place finishes this month; this is a better race but newly applied cheekpieces might eke out more.
runners.comment: Couple of third-place finishes off this mark of late; new cheekpieces might eke out more

runners.name: Invincible Boy
runners.spotlight: Very promising debut when 50-1 second in C&D novice (good) in October and heavy ground was a plausible excuse when he underwhelmed next time; scored with authority on his return in Pontefract novice (6f, good to soft) nearly twelve weeks ago; not seen since but this opening mark looks fair and he's much respected.
runners.comment: Good debut when 50-1 second in C&D novice; returned with a win; player on handicap debut

runners.name: Percy's Star
runners.spotlight: Stays this trip; displayed improved form on his handicap debut when making all to beat five others comfortably at Leicester (5f, good) in first-time cheekpieces (retained) 19 days ago; 8lb rise to deal with in a better race but he could be capable of better again and can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Improved in cheekpieces when winning last time; up 8lb in better race but he's in the mix

runners.name: Kinnalargy
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful 5f juvenile winner who reappeared having been gelded with a modest effort here in May; much better a fortnight later when fifth of 12 at Thirsk (also 5f, good); stays 6f and could step forward again.
runners.comment: Stays 6f; latest fifth (5f, good) was a step in the right direction; could build on that

runners.name: Vidmiyr
runners.spotlight: On the up since joining current yard, landing 7f Lingfield maiden (good) in May before he was beaten two short heads at Chester (7.5f, soft) a fortnight ago; runner-up was a convincing winner at Carlisle on Wednesday; up 2lb and he's a big player if this drop back down to 6f proves no barrier.
runners.comment: Running well and beaten two short heads (7.5f, soft) latest; player if 6f is no barrier
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Evening Blues
runners.spotlight: 1m AW maiden winner in November for Ger Lyons; encouraging stable debut when beaten just over 2l at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) in April but it all went wrong when racing far too freely over 1m1f three weeks ago; takes a sharp drop in trip.
runners.comment: Encouraging stable debut but raced far too freely last time; takes a sharp drop in trip

runners.name: Logi Bear
runners.spotlight: Didn't really build on a good start for Richard Hannon and he took a couple of goes to warm up for this yard; much better last time, however, when second of 13 at Thirsk (6f, good) three weeks ago; now he needs to kick on from that.
runners.comment: Bit of a revival when second last time; now he needs to build on that

runners.name: Million Gold
runners.spotlight: Nicely backed 100-30 shot when going in first time at Kempton in December but he was another to suffer an odds-on reversal on his second start when third at Newcastle (also 6f, AW) the following month; subsequent absence suggests there may have been an excuse and he's an interesting contender on this turf and handicap debut; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Won debut but odds-on reversal (also 6f AW) next time; cheekpieces on for turf/h'cap debut

runners.name: Von Trotter
runners.spotlight: Finished runner-up on his first three starts over 5f/6f on AW but his turf form isn't comparable, including when beaten 12l in a visor (discarded) last Friday; others hold more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Yet to show his best on turf; others hold more pressing claims

runners.name: Mr Noble
runners.spotlight: Sent off 5-4 when beating six rivals on his debut at Newcastle in December; that race contained subsequent winner but he was turned over at 8-15 when second on his return at Wolverhampton (again 6f, AW) this month; slightly caught napping that day and he brings plenty of potential to this turf/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won debut but odds-on 2nd (also 6f, AW) this month; brings potential to turf/h'cap debut

runners.name: Rikki Tiki Tavi
runners.spotlight: A good start to his career included a Thirsk maiden (5f, good to firm) win in August but his form tailed off (mitigating circumstances) subsequently and he was never a factor when beating one home on his return at Thirsk in April; off since.
runners.comment: Never a factor when beating one home on his return at Thirsk in April; off since

runners.name: Bleep Test
runners.spotlight: 6f Redcar maiden winner whose best form came when runner up here (5f, good to firm) and at Pontefract (6f, good) in June; not beaten far on her handicap debut at Hamilton the following month but subsequently absent; look for market support on this comeback.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on h'cap debut last July but subsequently absent; look for market support

runners.name: Arduis Invicta
runners.spotlight: She was dropped to this grade when running well for second at Musselburgh (5f, good) three weeks ago; same mark and stays this trip so she ought to be on the premises.
runners.comment: Stays 6f; second off this mark over 5f last time and she ought to be on the premises

races.race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Pearl Eye
runners.spotlight: Only his third run for Jim Goldie when scoring at Hamilton (1m1f, good) and off a 2lb rise he was a closing third over 1m1f here (good) when not assisted by his track position; bang in form and should run well.
runners.comment: Closing third over 1m1f here two weeks ago was probably a personal best; up 1lb

runners.name: Double Parked
runners.spotlight: Appeared to be found wanting for stamina over C&D two runs back but that was a warm handicap and he's since gone down narrowly in a Class 3 over 1m at Redcar (good; new tongue-tie), where he wasn't stopping; races up with the pace and firmly in the reckoning.
runners.comment: Wasn't stopping over 1m at Redcar latest and previous defeat over C&D was in a hot race

runners.name: Sisterandbrother
runners.spotlight: 2-6 for this yard but both wins were at 1m4f at Class 6 level; looks worth taking on, for all that his last race on the AW didn't go entirely to plan.
runners.comment: 2-6 for this yard but both wins were at 1m4f at Class 6 level; looks worth taking on

runners.name: Hengest
runners.spotlight: Progressive for this yard and was unbeaten in handicaps until an excusable defeat on rain-hit ground at Epsom; probably still on a fair mark and trip/ground will be ideal; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in handicaps until an excusable defeat on rain-hit ground at Epsom
runners.owner: Gee Gees Racing, P Gleeson & N Barnes

runners.name: No Knee Never
runners.spotlight: Thoroughly exposed 5yo who was in fair form until here last time when never involved from off the pace; has never won off this high a mark but what does bode well is that his last three wins have come in amateur races under Henry Callan who is back aboard for the first time since his last win in April; the cheekpieces also return after going in a new hood last time.
runners.comment: Last three wins have been under Henry Callan who returns for the first time since April

runners.name: Caph Star
runners.spotlight: 1m2f winner on good ground at Nottingham in April (4lb lower) but has dropped his guard the last twice and he's been contesting Class 5s, so this demands a fair bit more even if back in the groove.
runners.comment: He's been contesting Class 5s so this demands a fair bit more even if back in the groove

runners.name: Spirit Genie
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner on a handy mark but it's been a quiet start to this season and, even if back on song, looks vulnerable to stronger stayers over this far; the return of a visor might be no bad thing.
runners.comment: Quiet this season and even if back on song he looks vulnerable to stronger stayers

runners.name: Glistening Nights
runners.spotlight: This trip on fast ground suits and every chance with a repeat of his two runs at Ripon; took a backward step here two weeks ago but he has C&D form and could run well.
runners.comment: Two good runs at Ripon before disappointing here 15 days ago; has C&D form

runners.name: Urban Road
runners.spotlight: He's 8-50 on the AW and only 1-13 on turf; he was bang there over this far and off this mark at Redcar in April but he's another in here on a recovery mission after two lesser efforts.
runners.comment: Capable 7yo but he's another in here on a recovery mission after two lesser efforts
runners.owner: S McMillan, F Reay & A D Brown

runners.name: Spirit Catcher
runners.spotlight: Unlike him to miss the break as last time and had been running well from 1m1f to 1m4f; the concerns are that he does look high enough in the weights and this is tougher than usual.
runners.comment: Concerns are that he does look high enough in the weights and this is tougher than usual

runners.name: Ben Lawers
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m AW maiden in Ireland and was close up in a 1m Lingfield handicap three starts ago; however, not so good on his final two runs for Jessica Harrington and sold on for 5,000gns.
runners.comment: Respected on some of his form for Jessica Harrington but was sold on for 5,000gns

runners.name: Machete
runners.spotlight: Not as good as he was in France and last two runs have been well below his best; however, on too good a mark to dismiss and inmates from this yard can return to form from nowhere.
runners.comment: On too good a mark to dismiss and inmates from this yard can return to form from nowhere

runners.name: Midnight Strike
runners.spotlight: 0-14 since a successful debut for Joseph O'Brien; well down the field in all three runs for this yard, all at 7f; good bit to prove and he's unraced beyond a mile.
runners.comment: 0-14 since a successful debut for Joseph O'Brien; nowhere in 7f handicaps for this yard
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Theme Park
runners.spotlight: Potentially very well handicapped but that's for a reason as last season was mostly underwhelming; off since November.
runners.comment: Well handicapped for a reason as last season was mostly underwhelming

races.race_name: Barronstown Stud Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden

runners.name: Bull Shark
runners.spotlight: Has run respectably in four maidens with best effort a second to a highly-regarded Ballydoyle colt over C&D; well beaten in the 6f Coventry Stakes since but can do better back to this trip.
runners.comment: Second over C&D before well beaten in Coventry Stakes; do better back to this trip

runners.name: Giant Sequoia
runners.spotlight: Well-bred sort was made 4-9 favourite on debut over C&D but couldn't get to grips with a stablemate and Bull Shark; however, will surely be sharper with that run behind him and should take the beating today with Moore staying aboard.
runners.comment: Beaten by a stablemate and Bull Shark on debut; will be sharper with that run behind him

runners.name: Darivan
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam French 1m2f/10.5f winner (inc Listed; RPR 109), half-sister to useful 1m3f winner Deremah, out of 1m 2yo winning half-sister to French Derby winner Darsi; nicely bred but will appreciate further in time and likely to improve from this.
runners.comment: Nicely bred but will appreciate further in time and likely to improve from this
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Venetian Power
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to 10.6f AW winner Powerful Hook Head (RPR 84); dam German 6.5f 2yo winner (87), half-sister to winners Beauty Crescent (6f 2yo Listed) and Worship (useful 6f 2yo); nicely-bred sort has the credentials to run well on debut, so check the market.
runners.comment: Nicely-bred sort has the credentials to run well on debut so check the market

runners.name: Shakespeare
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam 7f-1m2f winner (inc AW/Group 1; RPR 120), half-sister to winners Speak In Colours (6f Group 2) and Pretty In Grey (useful 7f/1m); by a legendary sire and made 1,300,000gns as a yearling; seems yard third-string on debut but likely can run with promise.
runners.comment: Well-bred colt; seems yard third-string on debut but likely can run with promise

runners.name: Winnie's Wish
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in two maidens and will be of more interest when tackling handicaps.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in two maidens and will be of more interest when tackling handicaps

runners.name: Oklahoma
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; brother to 1m/1m1f winner Hawk Mountain (inc 2yo Futurity Group 1; RPR 119), half-brother to winners Wingspan (1m/9.4f inc Listed; 114) and Officer (7f 2yo/1m Listed; 112); dam 7f-1m4f winner (inc 2yo/dual Group 1; 119); superbly-bred colt looks the yard second-string but still respected.
runners.comment: Brother to Futurity Group 1 winner Hawk Mountain; looks yard second-string but respected
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Yellow Sky
runners.spotlight: Second foal; half-brother to 1m4f AW (RPR 80) and 2m1f hurdle winner Cinnamon Coco; dam unraced sister to 7f Group 3 winner Talaayeb; by a leading sire and cost E140,000 as a yearling; seems yard's first-string on jockey bookings and can run well.
runners.comment: Cost E140,000 as a yearling; seems yard's first-string on jockey bookings and can run well

races.race_name: Jebel Ali Racecourse And Stables Anglesey Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Confucius
runners.spotlight: Well-bred colt cost Gns1.7m as a yearling; just denied on debut over C&D on soft before winning with plenty to spare over 6f at Naas; cracking run in the Coventry Stakes behind a stablemate, not beaten far, and a repeat of that will make him difficult to beat.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in Coventry Stakes and a repeat of that will make him difficult to beat

runners.name: Immortal Guard
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on debut in a 6f maiden here before a fine effort in the Group 3 Marble Hill Stakes; was 2.5l behind the Coventry winner Great Barrier Reef then, so doesn't have much ground to make up on Confucius on collateral form.
runners.comment: Beaten 2.5l by Great Barrier Reef in the Marble Hill; not far behind Confucius on form
runners.owner: Ramiro Restrepo & Jacob West & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Ballinea Star
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably in decent maidens at Naas and Navan before getting off the mark at Cork over 6f; that form hasn't been tested since, but it is likely he will have to find significant improvement; seems yard's second-string.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Cork over 6f; likely he will have to find significant improvement

runners.name: Dandyman Dan
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner at Bath ran respectably in a Sandown Listed event next time; below form in softer ground in the Woodcote at Epsom; this surface will suit better, but needs to find a new level of form.
runners.comment: Below form on soft last time; this will suit better but needs to find a new level of form
runners.owner: Eric Griffiths, Mrs T Burns & Partner

runners.name: Mr Predicted
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to US stakes-placed 5.5f turf 2yo winner Lawyer Mason; dam twice-raced close relative of US 1m1f Grade 2 winner Adriano; $200,000 yearling made $300,000 as a 2yo; pitched in at the deep end on debut but sire was a top-class 2yo and has to be respected from this source.
runners.comment: Pitched in at the deep end on debut but and has to be respected from this source
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier, John C Oxley Et Al

runners.name: The Harv
runners.spotlight: A 260,000GBP breeze-up buy; got up late to win a Goodwood novice over 6f (soft) on debut; beaten 9l in the Coventry Stakes, but is better than that as he didn't have much racing room late on; can get closer to Confucius today.
runners.comment: Beaten 9l in the Coventry Stakes when didn't get a clear run; can get closer to Confucius

races.race_name: Neville Homes Summer Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)

runners.name: Aviatrice
runners.spotlight: Has won three times on the AW including a 1m Listed race at Dundalk last November; nearly took advantage of a lower turf mark when third on return in a 6f handicap at this track, but was below that form since at Cork; has to rebound.
