runners.comment: 3rd on return in a 6f handicap here but was below that form since over C&D; has to rebound

runners.name: Greydreambeliever
runners.spotlight: Won on 2yo debut for Karl Burke but failed to win again, albeit some decent runs; solid efforts in defeat all starts for this yard including twice denied by Rion Rubette at Gowran; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Solid in defeat for this yard incl' twice denied by Rion Rubette at Gowran; shortlisted
runners.owner: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck

runners.name: Mallavelly
runners.spotlight: Won three times last year, an AW maiden (1m) plus handicaps at Leopardstown (1m) and Tipperary (7.5f); outclassed in a Group 3 on return and probably found the ground too soft since; can do better on this faster ground.
runners.comment: Probably found the ground too soft last time; can do better on this faster ground

runners.name: Glory To Be
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f maiden on soft at Navan last autumn and doubled her tally in a 7f Leopardstown handicap (yld/sft) in April; below form twice since and this ground might be a bit too lively for her.
runners.comment: Dual winner but below form last twice and this ground might be a bit too lively for her

runners.name: Vega's Muse
runners.spotlight: Won for the second time when taking a 1m Leopardstown handicap on return; below that form at Leopardstown and Naas since; found to be lame after last time, so has to bounce back.
runners.comment: Below form last twice but was found to be lame after last time so has to bounce back

runners.name: Fingerpaint
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D maiden on debut last year and was a fine third in a Tipperary Group 3 next time after a 100-day break; didn't seem to handle heavy ground on final start last year but was again below her best on return at Navan recently; down in grade and cheekpieces could re-ignite the spark.
runners.comment: C&D winner; below her best on return at Navan; cheekpieces need to re-ignite the spark

runners.name: Bint Majestic Roi
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in a 6f handicap on soft here in April and followed up on good ground at Naas next time over the same trip; beaten 5l in a competitive 7f Cork handicap since and will have to do better now.
runners.comment: Two wins before beaten 5l in a competitive 7f Cork handicap and will have to do better now

runners.name: Rion Rubette
runners.spotlight: Has hit form lately, winning two 7f handicaps at Gowran on easy ground, beating Greydreambeliever on both occasions; raised 6lb for latter success and handles faster ground fine, so has to be on the shortlist with claimer staying aboard.
runners.comment: Won twice at Gowran lately; up 6lb and handles fast ground so has to be on the shortlist

runners.name: Meriden
runners.spotlight: Has shown improved form since tackling handicaps, winning at Down Royal (7f), here over 6.5f and Leopardstown back over 7f; up another 8lb, but handles this ground and has to be respected.
runners.comment: Has won last three; up another 8lb, but handles this ground and has to be respected

runners.name: She's Too Kool
runners.spotlight: Maiden owes her high mark to finishing fourth in the Group 3 Anglesey Stakes at this meeting last year; not a bad effort on her first run since at Fairyhouse last time and should improve from that; 1lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Fourth in the Group 3 Anglesey Stks at this meeting last year; has to improve from return

runners.name: Orchidaceae
runners.spotlight: Dual 2yo winner including over 6f at this track; won a Fairyhouse handicap last year over this trip but ran below that level twice after; has to be at her best on return from an absence.
runners.comment: Three-time winner was below-par final two runs last year and needs best on return

runners.name: Nancy J
runners.spotlight: Won a 2yo maiden on debut at Gowran for Jessica Harrington and some good runs in defeat after without winning; well beaten on debut for this stable over 6f here, but that was on testing ground and can do a lot better now back on faster.
runners.comment: Well beaten on debut for this stable over 6f here in testing ground but can do better now

races.race_name: Airlie Stud Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies)

runners.name: Sun Goddess
runners.spotlight: Looked in need of the experience when just denied on debut at Naas; was impressive when beating Green Empress by 5l over C&D next time; well backed when a close second in the Albany Stakes at Royal Ascot; is the one to beat if turning out fresh after just eight days rest.
runners.comment: Close second in the Albany Stakes; the one to beat if fresh after just eight days rest

runners.name: Cleodolinda
runners.spotlight: Both starts have been at Navan over 5f; improved from her debut second to go one better last time; the form of that race is no stronger than average, so plenty of improvement needed up in trip and grade.
runners.comment: Navan 5f winner last time; plenty of improvement needed up in trip and grade

runners.name: Controlla
runners.spotlight: Ran a cracker on debut to be beaten only a neck in a Naas Group 3 by the subsequent Queen Mary winner Victorious; was disappointing in the Windsor Castle Stakes 10 days ago but can be a player if resuming her progression.
runners.comment: Disappointing in the Windsor Castle Stakes but can be a player if resuming her progression

runners.name: Beibhinn
runners.spotlight: Was beaten 6l on debut by the Queen Mary winner Victorious; improved on that when winning a Listowel maiden over 6.5f, doing well after being pestered throughout; looks the stable second-string on riding arrangements, but could still run well.
runners.comment: Listowel maiden winner; looks the stable second-string but could still run well

runners.name: Belle Of The Ball
runners.spotlight: Improved nicely from her debut fifth over C&D to win a 5f Cork maiden on soft ground; is a speedy sort who is possibly better suited to 5f; a hood is added to help her relax; can go well but vulnerable.
runners.comment: Speedy sort is possibly better suited to 5f; a hood is added; can go well but vulnerable
runners.owner: OMEGA Racing & Belle Morris & Russell B Jones Jr

runners.name: Maggie Walker
runners.spotlight: A bargain yearling purchase at only Eur6,500; won a Cork auction maiden on debut over 6f on soft; the runner-up was since second to Cleodolinda at Navan; faster ground today but could go well if handling it.
runners.comment: Won a Cork auction maiden on debut over 6f on soft; solid form; go well if handling ground

runners.name: Green Empress
runners.spotlight: Did a fair bit wrong on her debut over C&D last month but still showed promise when a 5l second to the re-opposing Sun Goddess; should get closer to that rival today and looks the stable's selection of two here.
runners.comment: A 5l second to Sun Goddess on debut; can get closer to that rival today; stable selected

races.race_name: Paddy Power Pretty Polly Stakes (Group 1) (Fillies & Mares)

runners.name: Thundering On
runners.spotlight: By Frankel, dam Thundering Nights won this event in 2021; Group 3-placed on second start at two; beaten favourite in a maiden on seasonal debut before comfortable win in a Group 3 at Navan (1m2f, good to yielding) in April; confidently ridden before taking full command in the final furlong in the Oaks; will be hard to beat if the ground is not too quick.
runners.comment: A brilliant winner of the Oaks, will be hard to beat if the ground is not too quick

runners.name: Estrange
runners.spotlight: Very talented 5yo who raced exclusively over 1m4f in her four races last year; won at Group 2/3 level at Haydock before excellent form in defeat in two Group 1s, second to Minnie Hauk at York and to Kalpana at Ascot; started the new season brightly with odds-on win at Carlisle (1m3f); handled quick ground then, but reckoned to be best on a slower surface.
runners.comment: Enjoyed a fine 2025 campaign, started this term positively, best at 1m4f but a big player

runners.name: One Look
runners.spotlight: Course Group 3 winner over 1m last year, but seems most effective at 1m2f, judged on her placed efforts in the Blandford Stakes and the Prix de l'Opéra (just in front of Wemightakedlongway); later, in rear in Ascot race in which Estrange was second; should be better for her run behind shorter-priced stablemate City of Memphis over 1m here last month.
runners.comment: In rear in Ascot race in which Estrange was second, balance of form makes good reading

runners.name: Beautify
runners.spotlight: Group 2 winner 6f at this venue last summer and close second to Precise in the Moyglare; well beaten in the Cheveley Park, and down the field in the Nell Gwyn on seasonal debut; tackled 1m for the first time when sixth of 11 in the Irish 1,000 Guineas; plenty of stamina in the family, but hard to make a case for her.
runners.comment: Second in last year's Moyglare, sixth in Irish 1,000 Guineas, hard to make a case for her
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Red Letter
runners.spotlight: Winner of a Listed race at Killarney (1m, soft) and Group 3 at this venue (1m1f, good to yielding) last summer; creditable third of 12 in C&D Group 3 on reappearance in April; ran as well as could have been expected when second to the smart See The Fire in a four-runner Group 2 event at York last month.
runners.comment: Smart domestic form, good second in a four-runner Group 2 event at York last month

runners.name: Carolina Jetstream
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner on AW; 0-6 on turf but had performed well on her last two starts; 66-1, made much of the running when second of 12 in a 1m Group 2 at this venue last month, with One Look in fourth; raced prominently before fading into fifth (of 15) in the Duke Of Cambridge Stakes at Royal Ascot; suited by quick ground.
runners.comment: Has performed with credit in demanding company this season, set another stiff task now
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Qilin Queen
runners.spotlight: Progressive last term, landing a Listed event at Newbury (1m2f, good to firm) and the Group 2 Prix de Malleret at Longchamp (1m4f, good to soft); struggled in the Yorkshire Oaks and Prix de l'Opéra; again set a stiff task on reappearance at Longchamp; up against it.
runners.comment: Struggled in the Yorkshire Oaks and Prix de l'Opera after Group 2 win in France

runners.name: Nyra
runners.spotlight: Bought for E875,000 last December; 1-9 in Germany, winning a 1m3f Hamburg Group 3 (good) and better form when a close third in the German Oaks at Dusseldorf (1m3f, soft) and placed twice in two Group 2 races; good run over 1m4f at Cork on second run for Thundering On's trainer; plenty to prove at this level.
runners.comment: German import, stablemate of Thundering On, plenty to prove at this level

runners.name: Wemightakedlongway
runners.spotlight: Last season's best performances came in Group 1s, runner-up in the Irish Oaks (1m4f, good) and close fourth in the Prix de l'Opera at Longchamp (1m2f, very soft), just behind One Look; well held by Estrange on Ascot running; this season's form is unconvincing; visored now.
runners.comment: Well held by Estrange on Ascot running, this season's form is unconvincing, visored now

races.race_name: Reserve Ladies Derby Handicap

runners.name: Senna's Girl
runners.spotlight: 0-16 but placed five times; good runs back-to-back when fourth and third at Leopardstown and Limerick in April (both yielding); poor run on good ground on penultimate start, more like it on an easier surface last time; stable had a winner at Naas this week.
runners.comment: 0-16 record is a negative, placed five times, stable had a winner at Naas this week

runners.name: Rocket Robyn
runners.spotlight: Maiden whose best run last season was on heavy ground; unplaced in two handicaps this term and held by Great Mover based on a clash at Leopardstown.
runners.comment: Maiden, unplaced in two handicaps, held by Great Mover based on a clash at Leopardstown

runners.name: Diamond Exchange
runners.spotlight: Won AW maiden in January; returned to turf with good efforts in 1m2f handicaps at Leopardstown; soft ground may not have been ideal at the same venue on latest; good chance here for Sophie Carter who won this event in 2024 for Gordon Elliott.
runners.comment: Good form in 1m2f handicaps at Leopardstown, soft ground may not have been ideal last time
runners.owner: Bronsan Racing & H Redknapp & John Breslin

runners.name: Genoah
runners.spotlight: All four wins on AW; weak turf record (0-6) is a concern; booking of the very experienced Siobhan Rutledge is the main positive; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: All four wins on AW, weak turf record is a concern; jockey booking the main positive

runners.name: Great Mover
runners.spotlight: Only win from 22 starts came on heavy ground at Gowran last September; failed to retrieve her best form in her first four outings this term, including a Leopardstown race won by Diamond Exchange; placed twice at Leopardstown since then, both races run on soft; capable 7lb claimer
runners.comment: 1-22 record is a worry, decent form in two races since unplaced behind Diamond Exchange

runners.name: Cooley's Mist
runners.spotlight: Fourth of 21 on C&D handicap debut last summer was his best run for Natalia Lupini; no show on last month's Roscommon comeback; much better when a long-priced third at Leopardstown; relatively unexposed and could feature with 7lb off.
runners.comment: Long-priced third at Leopardstown, relatively unexposed and could feature with 7lb off

runners.name: Voice Of Reason
runners.spotlight: Much better record on AW (three wins from nine starts); 1-11 on turf and needs to improve on recent efforts; very capable claimer booked.
runners.comment: Better record on AW, needs to improve on recent efforts, very capable claimer booked

runners.name: Fixation
runners.spotlight: Won over the extended version of this trip at Dundalk last November on stable debut; second over 1m at the same venue on final start last year; resumed with another 1m second at Leopardstown; not beaten far in a 20-runner contest here last time, definite chance on overall form; cheekpieces fitted.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in a 20-runner contest here last time, definite chance on overall form

runners.name: Earthwatch
runners.spotlight: Sandown 1m handicap winner for William Haggas (1m, good); poor in a claimer and two maiden hurdles for new yard, though mishap when carried out early at Clonmel last time; cheekpieces added to tongue-tie worn since joining this yard.
runners.comment: Fit from hurdling, mishap last time, won over 1m on good ground in Britain last season

runners.name: Touch The Moon
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner on the Flat on good/good to firm ground; some useful runs in defeat over hurdles; something amiss over hurdles last time; started Flat campaign with a good run over 1m4f at Leopardstown.
runners.comment: Something amiss over hurdles last time, started Flat campaign with a good run over 1m4f

runners.name: Clear Quartz
runners.spotlight: Record of 6-37 on turf; best form at Galway, the scene of three of his wins; in contrast, he has a weak Curragh record, unplaced in eight visits; pleasing second at Leopardstown recently on seasonal debut; a reproduction would put him in the mix.
runners.comment: Best form at Galway, pleasing second at Leopardstown recently, weak record at this venue

runners.name: Musical Act
runners.spotlight: Won two Kempton novices at 7f/1m in 2023; close up in Grade 3 at Keeneland in April 2024; no better than fourth in eight runs (six on AW) since joining this yard; well held by Clear Quartz on Leopardstown running; hard to fancy despite having a top apprentice aboard.
runners.comment: Two wins at Kempton in 2023, modest Irish form, well held by Clear Quartz on recent form

runners.name: Ob La Di
runners.spotlight: Won over an extended 1m2f at Naas last August on good ground; not suited by heavy ground on her final two starts; in rear on soft on return; much better when fourth at Leopardstown but poor again at Gowran; stablemate Fixation appeals more.
runners.comment: Fair run on penultimate start, suited by good ground but stablemate Fixation appeals more

races.race_name: Fitzpatrick's Garage Group Handicap

runners.name: The Piper's Call
runners.spotlight: Useful form in two runs in decent company last summer (both over 6f; good); second of 13 at this venue; not quite as good but respectable nonetheless when third at Naas; coped with easier ground when making a winning reappearance here early this month; opening mark seems fair; strong chance.
runners.comment: Confirmed the promise of two juvenile runs with a course maiden win, obvious chance here

runners.name: Inishfallen
runners.spotlight: Proving hard to win with (1-25); not beaten far on last two AW runs in the spring; failed to make a significant impact in a Listowel race won by Unauthorized.
runners.comment: Proving hard to win with (1-25), failed to feature in a Listowel race won by Unauthorized
runners.owner: F J Carragher/Yorkavenue Syndicate/Park Ave Syndicate

runners.name: Joyful Tidings
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f Tipperary maiden on debut last year, beating a subsequent Listed winner; failed to make the improvement that might have been anticipated; best run this term came over 5f at Cork; not far behind Midnight Dusk and Nakamura when dead-heeating for fourth at Fairyhouse.
runners.comment: Only win was gained first time out, has shown potential for handicap success lately

runners.name: Morehampton
runners.spotlight: 7f winner at two and fair fifth in a 7f Listed contest at Fairyhouse; this season's form has been uninspiring; perhaps cheekpieces will help his cause; others much preferred, including stablemate Nakamura.
runners.comment: Uninspiring form this season, stable appears to have a better chance with Nakamura

runners.name: Midnight Dusk
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Dundalk maiden in December and solid runs next three starts, 5f and 6f; poor Navan run in May possibly came too soon after his previous start; showed useful form in making all to beat Nakamura at Fairyhouse; not much between the pair and Joyful Tidings who dead-heated for fourth.
runners.comment: Showed useful form in making all to beat Nakamura at Fairyhouse, not much between the pair

runners.name: Real Encounter
runners.spotlight: Narrowly beaten in a 6f Dundalk maiden in April before winning over 5f there 13 days later; definite merit in handicap debut at Naas last month, close up until reportedly hitting a ridge over a furlong down, before rallying; went close over the minimum trip here last time; worth considering.
runners.comment: 5f AW maiden winner, went close over the minimum trip here last time, worth considering

runners.name: Fox In Flight
runners.spotlight: Finished last on his first two starts this term; gave a reassuring display when second over 5f at Cork; winner Sarahmae franked the form with an emphatic follow-up success over the same trip at Down Royal.
runners.comment: Finished last on his first two starts this term, gave a reassuring display over 5f at Cork

runners.name: Nakamura
runners.spotlight: Was busy at two, winning nurseries at Navan and Cork over this trip; below his best this term until returning to form to take second place behind a front-running winner Midnight Dusk at Fairyhouse; took him a while to get into the clear then; leading contender.
runners.comment: Returned to form with second behind Midnight Dusk at Fairyhouse, can feature again

runners.name: Valiant Force
runners.spotlight: Sole win on turf in the 2023 Norfolk Stakes; has won five conditions races at Dundalk since, the most recent over 5f in February; has struggled since then; drop in class after contesting a Listed race and C&D Group 2 in May; was 20lb higher at his peak; still an entire.
runners.comment: 2023 Norfolk Stakes winner, five AW wins, was 20lb higher at his peak; still an entire
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Unauthorized
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Leopardstown maiden at two; respectable effort on comeback in 3yo handicap at this venue, weakening late over 7f; won with plenty to spare over a little further than this when apprentice-ridden at Listowel; up 7lb and no claim this time, may be vulnerable.
runners.comment: Won with plenty to spare at Listowel, up 7lb and no claim this time, may be vulnerable

runners.name: Mary Shoelaces
runners.spotlight: Two of her three wins have come over 5f at this track; has finished last on both starts this term; hard to fancy against that background, despite her liking for this course.
runners.comment: Two of her three wins have come over 5f at this track, in rear on both starts this term

runners.name: Bishopton
runners.spotlight: Progressed well last year over 7f, winning twice; resumed progress on return in March when defying top-weight at Dundalk; towards the rear over C&D before more like his best when fifth of 15 over 7f at Cork 13 days ago; probably lacks the pace of several of the 6f winners in this field.
runners.comment: More effective at 7f, probably lacks the pace of several of the 6f winners here
runners.owner: W Jones/D W Sloan/M C Mocchetti/Kabin Racing Syndicate

runners.name: Clonmacash
runners.spotlight: Much stronger record on AW (5-30, placed nine times) as opposed to turf (1-24, placed four times); his run at Navan over 6f on his penultimate start was one of his better efforts on grass.
runners.comment: Has a much better strike rate on AW, modest record on turf makes him a risky proposition

runners.name: Sommelier
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner; highlight of British form when second to Big Evs in a Listed race at York in May 2024; rated 99 at his peak. gained his first turf success off 72 when making all in a 20-runner contest over a little further than this here early this month; fair fifth under a fixed penalty in a 5f Cork event four days later; has gone up 11lb in total.
runners.comment: Recorded a first turf win at this venue early this month, fair run at Cork since, up 11lb

runners.name: Go Athletico
runners.spotlight: Nine-time turf winner, including a C&D Group 3 in 2023, Listed contests and a C&D premier handicap last July; was once rated 108, so has dropped a long way; positive third at Naas in April (6f, good); no luck in running over C&D last month; well below his best over 7f at Cork last time.
runners.comment: Nine-time turf winner, effective in smart company at his peak, has come down in the world

races.race_name: Jo Browne Luxury Skincare Irish EBF Maiden

runners.name: Suspicious Mindz
runners.spotlight: Best run was when third to a pair of solid stakes-level performers in a Listed contest last year; placed in 1m maidens this year, looked awkward under pressure when odds-on at Leopardstown; not one of his better efforts last time; 6f might be inadequate, but still of some interest, with cheekpieces and a 7lb claimer.
runners.comment: Not one of his better efforts last time, solid overall credentials, 6f might be inadequate

runners.name: Solana Beach
runners.spotlight: Promising debut second at Gowran (7f, soft); raced freely when fourth at Navan (1m, good) last month; now dropped in trip after a satisfactory third behind a pair of year-older rivals in a 7f handicap at Gowran; looks best of the fillies, should not be far away.
runners.comment: Fair maiden form, placed in a handicap, looks best of the fillies, should not be far away

runners.name: Focaccia
runners.spotlight: Has to bounce back from a poor handicap run; not as appealing as stablemate Solana Beach who would have to give her 6lb in a handicap.
runners.comment: Has to bounce back from a poor handicap run, not as appealing as stablemate Solana Beach

runners.name: Tide Of Fortune
runners.spotlight: Plenty of merit in a debut run that saw him finish eighth of 24 at this venue; went very close here on his second start; has obvious prospects of going one better in this rematch with Trek Home (fourth) and Starborn Legend (sixth).
runners.comment: Went very close here on his second start, has obvious prospects of going one better

runners.name: Amerilis
runners.spotlight: Ran twice for Paddy Twomey at two; made a good start for Gillian Scott when fourth of 12 in a C&D maiden in March, running on well in the closing stages; improved from handicap debut when second to an in-form rival here last time; can win a handicap off current mark, prospects of landing a maiden are slim.
runners.comment: Does not need to improve much to win a handicap, prospects of landing a maiden are slim

runners.name: Trek Home
runners.spotlight: Fourth on both starts, over 5f at Navan and C&D; on the latter form, has much the same chance as Starborn Legend and Tide Of Fortune in theory; rider described him as running green; improvement on the cards, but the same could be said of the other pair.
runners.comment: Fourth on both starts, has much the same chance as Starborn Legend and Tide Of Fortune
runners.owner: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan

runners.name: Dandymaria
runners.spotlight: Positives can be taken from her debut at Down Royal, though plenty of improvement is required, since she was beaten only 6l in fourth behind a 79-rated rival; Colin Keane takes the ride.
runners.comment: Positives can be taken from her debut at Down Royal, Colin Keane takes the ride

runners.name: Forza Magico
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; filly by Aclaim, sister to winners Anatoli (6f AW 2yo; RPR 85), Forza Toro (7f AW 2yo; 84) and East Bank (5f turf 2yo/7f AW; 71); dam 7f 2yo winner, out of 7f Group 3 winning sister to 6f Group 2 winner Haatef; worth noting that her three full siblings all won early in their careers.
runners.comment: Filly by Aclaim, worth noting that her three full siblings all won early in their careers

runners.name: Off We Go
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister by Cotai Glory to two winners; dam, by Tagula, an unraced sister to a 5f winner; cheap yearling; stable has had three winners in the last fortnight.
runners.comment: Half-sister by Cotai Glory to two winners, stable has had three wins in the last fortnight

runners.name: Saint Acclaim
runners.spotlight: Half-brother by St Mark's Basilica to several winners, including a US Grade 3 scorer, and Greatest Drama, a 7f winner also Listed-placed over 6f for this stable; dam, by Acclamation, won in Britain and the US; hooded for debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother by St Mark's Basilica to several winners including a US Grade 3 scorer

runners.name: Lilli's Angel
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly, first foal out of a 6f winner by Iffraaj; dam is a half-sister to three winners who were most effective at middle distances, out of an unraced close relation to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Walk On Bye; in good hands.
runners.comment: Sioux Nation filly, first foal out of a 6f winner by Iffraaj, in good hands

runners.name: Cara Teaghlaigh
runners.spotlight: Filly by Profitable; first foal out of an unraced half-sister to an Italian 1m Listed winner; cheap yearling; not a likely contender on debut.
runners.comment: By Profitable first foal out of an unraced mare, cheap yearling, not a likely contender

runners.name: Starborn Legend
runners.spotlight: E29,000 Y, E330,000 Breeze Up purchase; had a run in a barrier trial but still looked a bit raw when sixth in an 18-runner contest over C&D early in the month; beaten only a little over 2l, and has fair prospects of turning the tables on runner-up Tide Of Fortune and fourth-placed Trek Home.
runners.comment: May improve enough to reverse form in a renewal of rivalry with Tide Of Fortune/Trek Home

runners.name: Spinola Bear
runners.spotlight: E28,000 yearling; fourth foal; by Kodi Bear out of an Iffraaj mare who won in France; best watched on debut.
runners.comment: By Kodi Bear out of an Iffraaj mare who won in France, best watched on debut

runners.name: Lady Moon
runners.spotlight: Not a bad run in mid-division on debut; safely held by three of the likely key players who reached the first six in that event; tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Not a bad run in mid-division on debut, safely held by several of these rivals
runners.owner: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver

runners.name: Mountain Memory
runners.spotlight: Has shown some ability in two outings on soft, a possible handicap prospect in due course.
runners.comment: Has shown some ability in two outings on soft, a possible handicap prospect in due course

runners.name: Banshof
runners.spotlight: 260,000gns yearling; unraced for the Crisfords; picked up cheaply last October; failed to make a significant impact on debut at Dundalk.
runners.comment: Expensive yearling, cheap Godolphin cast-off, needs to improve from debut on AW

runners.name: Forbidden
runners.spotlight: 100-1 when finishing towards the back on debut at this venue early this month.
runners.comment: 100-1 when finishing towards the back on debut at this venue early this month

runners.name: Footstepstoglory
runners.spotlight: Placed over hurdles, two Flat runs this month, possibly one for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Placed over hurdles, two Flat runs this month, possibly one for handicaps after this

runners.name: Hazrama
runners.spotlight: Has had plenty of time to mature since her sole start at two; one to keep an eye on for future reference, rather than a betting proposition.
runners.comment: Has had plenty of time to mature since her sole start at two, one to keep an eye on
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Leannespour
runners.spotlight: Towards the back in outings at Navan and this venue last autumn; needs this for a handicap mark.
runners.comment: Towards the back in two outings last autumn, needs this for a handicap mark

races.race_name: Laurent-Perrier EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Stardom Glory
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two 6f contests at York (good), last time finishing 2l behind an odds-on Godolphin newcomer; plum draw; sets the standard.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two 6f contests at York; has the plum draw and should take all the beating

runners.name: The Dancing Pirate
runners.spotlight: Similar level of form when fourth in a Newmarket novice (5f, good to firm; 15-8 favourite) and in a conditions event at Beverley (5f, good to firm); may improve for the step up to 6f.
runners.comment: Fourth in a Newmarket novice and conditions event at Beverley (5f); may improve for 6f

runners.name: Deputy Vice
runners.spotlight: Had the best draw when third in a C&D novice on debut (good; 11-1); didn't run as well when last of four on softer ground at Carlisle next time; a possible but not ideally drawn.
runners.comment: Sound C&D 3rd on debut; not so good on softer going last time; a possible; not well drawn

runners.name: Trust Havana
runners.spotlight: Filly by Havana Grey who has been well beaten in two C&D novice events (good, good to soft); others stronger.
runners.comment: Havana Grey filly; well beaten in two C&D novice events; others stronger

runners.name: Walt
runners.spotlight: Cost 82,000gns at the breeze-up; well beaten fifth of six in a Haydock novice on last month's debut (6f, soft 13-2); needs to improve on this faster ground.
runners.comment: 82,000gns breeze-up buy; well beaten fifth on soft ground on debut; needs to improve

runners.name: Dreamlover
runners.spotlight: Gelding by Cotai Glory; fair fourth in a Southwell novice on debut (5f, 13-8 favourite); has since been gelded and is should improve as a result.
runners.comment: Fair fourth when favourite on debut on AW last month; gelded since and now blinkered

races.race_name: Absolut Tabasco Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: El Nay
runners.spotlight: Placed on all five starts (first two on the AW) last time getting caught on the line when short-head second at Leicester (1m, good; made the running); drops in trip (has been third over 7f, but also tried at 1m2f); contender.
runners.comment: Placed on all five starts, first two on AW, latest a short-head second over 1m; contender

runners.name: Eagle Bay
runners.spotlight: Confirmed the promise of his debut third at Catterick last October (7f, good; trained by Ed Walker) when getting off the mark in a Thirsk maiden earlier this month (7f, good to soft; beat a subsequent handicap winner by 5l); stiffer task here, but should go well.
runners.comment: Ex-Ed Walker; beat subsequent winner by 5l in Thirsk maiden last month; contender

runners.name: Remi Mae
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a valuable Haydock novice on second start last September (6f, good); didn't progress on the AW and was well beaten 11th of 12 in a decent Newcastle handicap last time (6f); return to 7f should suit.
runners.comment: Second at Haydock last September, didn't progress on the AW; step back up 7f should suit

runners.name: Albemagic
runners.spotlight: Well-related; beaten 22l in a Carlisle novice on last month's debut (7f, soft; 33-1); should improve, particularly on this better ground.
runners.comment: Well beaten 33-1 shot in Carlisle novice (soft); should improve on this better ground

races.race_name: Manor Car Hire Handicap

runners.name: Ruby's Angel
runners.spotlight: Did well to overcome stall 12 over C&D (good) at the end of May; failed to fire in a valuable 3yo handicap at York (22 runners) a fortnight ago but a lot better can be expected up against her elders in a less competitive race, particularly as she's bagged the inside stall.
runners.comment: Overcame stall 12 over C&D in May and has fared much better with the draw here; player

runners.name: Rosenpur
runners.spotlight: 6yo who posted career-best performance when dominating from the front over C&D (good) last month; used up energy to get across to lead from a wide draw when fifth of ten over 5f here a fortnight ago and can be treated as if still in good form.
runners.comment: Dominated from  front over C&D last month; better than result over 5f latest; respected

runners.name: Purest Time
runners.spotlight: Prevailed at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month, proving suited by the return to turf and belatedly registering a first win since joining Ian Williams; a 3lb rise still leaves him feasibly handicapped on the best of last year's form.
runners.comment: First win in Britain at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month; 3lb rise fair

runners.name: Dark Thirty
runners.spotlight: Ex-Richard Hannon; off the mark for the Quinn team when making virtually all in a 22-runner handicap at York Dante meeting (6f, good) in May and bounced straight back from a lesser run at Thirsk when 2l second of seven at Ripon (6f, good) nine days ago; same mark this afternoon; bold show likely from a handy draw.
runners.comment: Arrives in form and handily drawn for a prominent racer; reasons to be hopeful

runners.name: Candonomore
runners.spotlight: A ready winner at Bath (1m, firm) in April (final start for Stuart Williams); good start for Jennie Candlish when third over 1m1f at Ayr (good to firm) but not in the same form when beaten favourite at Carlisle (1m) ten days later; quite a strong traveller over those trips but whether he has the speed for sprinting remains to be seen.
runners.comment: All 31 starts over 7f+ so whether he has the speed for sprinting remains to be seen

runners.name: We Never Stop
runners.spotlight: Seems best on the AW these days and has run poorly on his last three turf outings; his hopes hinge on a recent wind operation having a positive effect.
runners.comment: Has run poorly on turf in recent months and recent wind op needs to make difference

runners.name: Stormy Impact
runners.spotlight: Won the 3yo Dash at Epsom (5f, good) last June and left reappearance behind when good fourth of 20 in the Dash there (5f, good to soft) three weeks ago; just as effective at 6f and can play a prominent role from an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Good fourth in Dash at Epsom latest; bold show likely from same mark

runners.name: Stratusnine
runners.spotlight: Finished strongly and nearly got up from a difficult position in 5f contest at York (good) last month; respectable eighth of 28 when stepped back up to 7f for the Buckingham Palace at Royal Ascot (good to firm) last week; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Went close over 5f at York last month and fair effort over 7f Ascot last week; claims

runners.name: Solar Aclaim
runners.spotlight: Back to form to take advantage of a reduced mark at Hamilton (5f, good to soft) ten days ago; put back up 8lb and has gained all six wins on going softer than good.
runners.comment: Did it readily at Hamilton latest but 8lb higher now and softer ground ideal

races.race_name: Queen Charlotte Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Copacabana Sands
runners.spotlight: Hooded when winning Naas Listed event (good) and Leopardstown Group 3 (soft) over 7f for Michael O'Callaghan last year; below form tackling firmer ground when fifth in 7f Lingfield Group 3 on return for Andrew Balding (same owner); good chance if recapturing her best.
runners.comment: Below par on yard debut but this Listed/Group 3 winner will have big say if back to best

runners.name: La Brodeuse
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced French winner (1m, soft) who made a cracking start for hew new connections when runner-up in the Group 3 Chartwell at Lingfield (7f, good to firm) last month, with reopposing Copacabana Sands and Dash Of Azure behind her; not in the same form when fourth to Circe (reopposing stablemate Magic Basma second) in Listed race at Musselburgh (7f, good) three weeks ago; needs to recapture the Lingfield form.
runners.comment: Second in Lingfield Group 3 on yard debut but behind two of these at Musselburgh since
runners.owner: Mrs R G Hillen And Partner

runners.name: Magic Basma
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has shown improved form in Listed races this term, finishing runner-up at Haydock (6f, good to soft) and Musselburgh (7f, good) on last two outings; Circe was 1.25l ahead of her at the latter but she's 3lb better off with Richard Hannon's charge this time; each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Listed races at Haydock (6f) and Musselburgh (7f); each-way claims

runners.name: Bellarchi
runners.spotlight: This 5yo is in the form of her life at present, gaining a third Musselburgh handicap win of the season when easily seeing off seven rivals 37 days ago; had a wide stall as a an excuse for a slightly below-par effort over the extended 7f here prior to that and she went close here last season; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: In form of life with three Musselburgh handicaps this year; can make mark in higher grade

runners.name: Dash Of Azure
runners.spotlight: Just failed to win a 7f Ascot Listed event (soft; unlucky) last autumn but in October but she needs to put a couple of underwhelming efforts this year behind her.
runners.comment: Went close in 7f Ascot Listed race last autumn but two below-par efforts this year

runners.name: Circe
runners.spotlight: Back to best when defeating the reopposing Magic Basma (La Brodeuse fourth, Brazilian Rose fifth) in 7f Musselburgh Listed race (good) on reappearance three weeks ago; more will be needed to defy a penalty but should be able to get a good position from her inside stall.
runners.comment: Beat a few of these in similar race at Musselburgh this month but carries 3lb penalty
runners.owner: Pineapple Stud And Miss C Douglas

runners.name: Brazilian Rose
runners.spotlight: Four handicap wins on the Southwell AW this January-March (7f/6f); ran right up to her best when 3.5l fifth of seven to Circe in 7f Musselburgh Listed race latest but it underlines she needs more at this level; a wide draw won't make it any easier.
runners.comment: Four AW wins but yet to win on turf and beaten by Circe at Musselburgh latest

runners.name: Royalty Bay
runners.spotlight: Back on form to take advantage of a reduced mark in 7f Musselburgh handicap (good) three weeks ago; this is harder but the fact she was second in a Group 3 in France as a juvenile provides some hope.
runners.comment: Took advantage of a reduced mark at Musselburgh latest but this demands more

runners.name: Beaujolais Nouveau
runners.spotlight: Returned to her best when winning a handicap with something to spare in first-time cheekpieces at Ripon (6f, good) in May; just as good when close fifth of 13 at York (6f, good again) 15 days ago; stays 7f but her form is short of the required standard at this level.
runners.comment: Arrives in good nick but her form is short of the standard needed at this level

races.race_name: Katie O'Brien's Irish Taverns Handicap

runners.name: The Resdev Scholar
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick at Doncaster (6f, heavy; also won on good) in November and continued in very good form when placed in four handicaps this season, including second over 7.5f here (soft) 15 days ago; should be thereabouts again.
runners.comment: In form with four places this season, including second here last time; respected
runners.owner: N Bradley 29, Resdev & South Bank Racing

runners.name: First Time
runners.spotlight: Left his stable debut well behind when sixth of 22 in traditionally strong York handicap (6f, good) a fortnight ago; the way he kept on in the closing stages suggests this return to 7f may suit; considered.
runners.comment: Kept on for sixth of 22 in strong 6f York handicap latest and return to 7f may suit

runners.name: Daydreama
runners.spotlight: Has a very mixed record across his 11 starts; good second in 7f handicap at the York Dante meeting in May but has failed to reproduce it in two subsequent outings.
runners.comment: Good second at York in May but below par twice since, taking record to 0-11

runners.name: Cool Molly
runners.spotlight: A four-time AW winner (from 5f to 7f) this year and ran really well when third over 7.5f here (good) in May; too keen when well held in big-field Goodwood handicap (7f) but looked back in form when sixth of 12 at York (6f, good) 15 days ago; drawn widest.
runners.comment: Four AW wins this year but course third in May shows she's just as good on turf

runners.name: Bright Summer
runners.spotlight: Built upon the promise of her debut fourth at Newbury (7f, good) in April when winning maiden at Lingfield (7f, AW) last month; had soft ground as excuse for a disappointing run under a penalty at Salisbury 18 days ago; retains unexposed potential now handicapping.
runners.comment: AW maiden win last month and presumably unsuited by soft ground since; handicap debut

runners.name: Astrazar
runners.spotlight: First start beyond 6f when winning Ffos Las maiden (7.3f, heavy) last September and followed up in last-gasp fashion in valuable EBF Final at York (7f, good) in October; never involved in two competitive handicaps this season but he met trouble on the first occasion and a slow start didn't help last time; will need to break better if he's to take advantage of his inside stall.
runners.comment: On the up last autumn but never involved in two competitive handicaps this term

runners.name: Sovereign Ocean
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced sort who made a winning start for Marco Botti in 7f Wolverhampton handicap on Boxing Day; faded as if possibly needing his first outing since when sixth of ten at Sandown (7f, good) 14 days ago; entitled to come on for the run.
runners.comment: Won AW handicap in December and possibly needed recent turf return; remains unexposed

runners.name: Start Me Up
runners.spotlight: First run since being gelded when off the mark in 6f handicap here (soft) 15 days ago; led late on in a style which suggests he should be as effective back at 7f and the Richard Spencer stable continues in excellent form.
runners.comment: First run since being gelded when winning over 6f here recently; yard continues in form

runners.name: Do Bronxs
runners.spotlight: No wins since a 10-1 success on his debut at Ayr a year ago but he made a bold bid from the front when second in a quite valuable handicap over 7.5f here (good to soft) a fortnight ago; same mark today and dangerous if able to get loose on the front end again.
runners.comment: Led for long way in useful 7.5f course handicap two weeks ago; contender from same mark

races.race_name: Castillo De Ibiza EBF Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: City Queen
runners.spotlight: Has won two of her last three races, the first at Southwell, the latest at Sandown (good); both were over 1m, but bred to be much better suited by 1m2f and further improvement likely.
runners.comment: Has won two of her last three races, both over 1m; bred to be suited by this trip; chance

runners.name: Hoseki
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; off the mark when beating a subsequent winner in a Redcar maiden last month (1m2f, good to firm); further progress likely; chance on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Unexposed; won Redcar maiden last month; further progress likely; chance on h'cap debut

runners.name: Winged One
runners.spotlight: Won a five-runner Newcastle novice last October; highly tried on first two starts this term, but beaten 7l when second in C&D handicap on soft ground last time (winner won since); cheekpieces now tried; each-way chance.
runners.comment: AW novice winner; 7l second in C&D h'cap latest; headgear now tried; each-way chance

runners.name: Bearin Up
runners.spotlight: Has won two of her last three races, both at around 1m on good, at Newmarket and Hamilton; 3lb higher than last time; not sure the step back up to 1m2f is ideal, although fair run on previous try at the trip.
runners.comment: Has won two of her last three races, both over 1m on good; not sure return to 1m2f ideal

runners.name: Patagonia Girl
runners.spotlight: Won twice last year, the first on good to firm at Salisbury, the second over C&D in June (good); also successful over 1m4f here in May; below-par in a Group 3 at Carlisle last time, but could bounce back.
runners.comment: Two wins last term, the second over C&D; also won over 1m4f here in May;  contender

runners.name: Model Yuko
runners.spotlight: Made all to win Wolverhampton maiden last June (8.5f) and was a close third in a Goodwood handicap for fillies next time (1m2f, good); first run on fast ground and first run of the season, but could go well.
runners.comment: Won AW maiden and 3rd in Goodwood h'cap last term; lacks a recent run but could go well

runners.name: Likealot
runners.spotlight: Won two of her nine races last year, both on the AW, the second by 3.75l when stepped up to 1m4f on final start (cheekpieces on for second time); also has decent form on turf; could go well, but better over 1m4f, lacks a recent run and on a career-high mark.
runners.comment: Two AW wins in 2025 (1m2f, 1m4f); a possible but better at 1m4f and on career-high mark

runners.name: Ciara Pearl
runners.spotlight: Won a Nottingham handicap over 1m2f on third start last season (good to firm); several good runs without winning after that; lacks a recent run and seems high enough in the ratings at present.
runners.comment: Conditions suit but lacks a recent run and is high enough in the ratings at present

races.race_name: Roxy Ball Room Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Venezuelan
runners.spotlight: Won a small-field maiden when with Ralph Beckett for whom he was competitive in handicaps at up to 1m4f; left that yard for 90,000gns; finished behind Midnight Rumble here four weeks ago on yard debut when trying 2m for the first time; that was after a lengthy break so might well be all the sharper here, and Toby Moore is a positive booking.
runners.comment: Behind Midnight Rumble here four weeks ago on yard debut when trying 2m for the first time

runners.name: Solar Pass
runners.spotlight: Both wins on the AW but runner-up in five of her seven races on the grass, the last two over C&D; clearly on a high enough mark but looks sure to give her running.
runners.comment: Runner-up here last two races; tough mark but looks sure to give her running

runners.name: Midnight Rumble
runners.spotlight: Much respected on his form for Alan King, for all that he was 1-22 for that yard; following a 30,000gns sale he ran okay over C&D on last month's stable debut, but no more than that.
runners.comment: 1-22 for Alan King; ran okay here on stable debut but no more than that

runners.name: Shipshape
runners.spotlight: Has won three of his last four and doesn't win by far so might still be ahead of the game mark-wise; stays 1m6f well so this far shouldn't be an issue, nor the track as his win two weeks ago was here (1m4f).
runners.comment: 1m4f winner two weeks ago, making it 3-4 since April; could still be on a useful mark

runners.name: Curran
runners.spotlight: Ex-Charlie Johnston for whom he finished third in this last year (2lb higher); joined Rod Millman for 6,000gns; tailed off on yard debut but always in the van when battling on best at Bath (1m6f, good); only 2lb higher but he's up into a Class 4 now.
runners.comment: Only 2lb higher than for his battling Bath win but he's up into a Class 4 now

runners.name: Furhaan
runners.spotlight: Successful handicap debut for Ed Walker off 2lb higher last September (extended 1m3f, heavy); well short of that level in three runs for his new connections; likely best watched for now.
runners.comment: Well short of his winning level in three runs for current connections

runners.name: Ruler Legend
runners.spotlight: Has been running well, and winning, over hurdles so fitness won't be an issue on this return to Flat action and has the form to feature off this mark; versatile ground-wise and there are no stamina issues.
runners.comment: Has the form to feature off this mark and he's been running very well over hurdles

races.race_name: Schweppes Cherry Pepper Handicap

runners.name: Condotti
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; best turf run for a while when staying-on third over 8.5f at Hamilton last time (good to soft); takes a marked step up in trip.
runners.comment: 0-10; best turf run for a while when staying-on third last time (8.5f); now tried 1m4f

runners.name: Relentless Hero
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo; off the mark in a Lingfield maiden last time (1m2f; soft); this step up in trip may well suit him and could go well on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo; Lingfield maiden win latest (1m2f); 1m4f should suit; chance on h'cap debut

runners.name: Oman
runners.spotlight: His five wins include two here, the second over this trip on fast ground in May 2024; versatile, though, for his latest win was over 1m1f at Goodwood last month (good to soft) and last run was when fourth over 7.5f here (soft); chance back up in trip.
runners.comment: Has won twice here (1m4f/1m6f); latest win was on 1m1f; chance back up in trip

runners.name: L'Eagle Aid
runners.spotlight: Has won three races, the latest over C&D off this mark last July (good); just fair form this term, last time finishing 5l sixth to Patagonia Girl (runs in the 4.45) over C&D (good).
runners.comment: C&D winner last July; only fair form this term, including last time when fifth over C&D

runners.name: Spartan Times
runners.spotlight: Went up 29lb for his four wins last season; wasn't at his best on first Flat run this year when last of five at Ripon (had been running well over hurdles); bit to prove.
runners.comment: Went up 29lb for four wins last season; below-par after jumps campaign on reappearance

runners.name: Percy Jones
runners.spotlight: Has won four times on the AW but is 0-23 on turf, although has been placed on 12 occasions; decent third at Bath last time (1m6f, good); each-way chance; cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Has won four times on AW, but 0-23 on turf; fair 3rd at Bath last time; cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Atheneum
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; eight-race maiden; hasn't run to his best on the AW for Phil Kirby, but may fare better back on turf.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; 0-8; not been at his best on AW for new yard; may fare better back on turf

runners.name: Triple Force
runners.spotlight: Has won four of his 17 turf races at up to 1m2f; respectable 2.75l third of 18 at York last time (1m2.5f good); interesting, although pulled hard and didn't seem to get home on only previous try at 1m4f.
runners.comment: Turf record is 4-17; comes here after good 4th at York last time (1m2.5f); 1m4f a concern

runners.name: Taritino
runners.spotlight: Has won five on the Flat at up to 1m6f; off the mark for this Flat season when winning at Salisbury by 4.5l 13 days ago; up 6lb but a contender.
runners.comment: Wide margin winner at Salisbury 13 days ago and should go well again despite 6lb rise

races.race_name: Total Task Consultancy Prosperity Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Buckland Belle
runners.spotlight: Advanced her form on her turf debut when gaining a first win in C&D handicap in May; backed it up with a good third of 12 over C&D 19 days ago so she's a player off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner; good third over C&D 19 days ago; merits serious consideration off same mark

runners.name: My Old Mate
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett gelding who is on the up since sent handicapping, beaten just a head when second (Buckland Belle a neck behind) over C&D 19 days ago; a player nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: On the up in h'caps, head second over C&D 19 days ago; a player nudged up 1lb

runners.name: She's Crafty
runners.spotlight: Is 0-12 and she failed to build on earlier promise when only eighth of nine in 7f Leicester handicap last month; not proving the easiest to catch right.
runners.comment: Is 0-12 and only eighth in Leicester h'cap last month; not proving easiest to catch right

runners.name: Shes Got The Blues
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in 7f classified event at Leicester and quickly followed up in similar company at Brighton (7f) six days ago; a bit more is needed here back in handicap company under a 6lb penalty but she still must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Won classified events at Leicester and Brighton; bit more needed in h'cap under 6lb pen

runners.name: Labiche
runners.spotlight: Tasleet filly who got back on track with third of eight in 7f Wolverhampton handicap 25 days ago, despite racing freely; not out of things eased 1lb with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Back on track with Wolverhampton third latest; not out of things with cheekpieces added
runners.owner: T A Frost & Simon Broke & Partners

runners.name: Lapidarist
runners.spotlight: Yet to score but she posted a good second in 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap and wasn't seen to best effect when sixth in 8.5f handicap there later in March; possibilities after a break.
runners.comment: Not seen to best effect with Wolverhampton sixth in March; possibilities after a break

races.race_name: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Divine Whisper
runners.spotlight: 9-1, made a highly promising start when second of nine in novice at York (5f, good) 15 days ago, running on well and just failing; this daughter of Mehmas is very much the one to beat.
runners.comment: Highly promising start when second at York, just failing; very much the one to beat

runners.name: Cash Flow Boy
runners.spotlight: Ten Sovereigns gelding; dam, unraced close relative of 1m 2yo Listed winner Classic Legend, is a half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Jallota; the market can guide for this newcomer.
runners.comment: Ten Sovereigns gelding; the market can guide for this newcomer

runners.name: Nascent Star
runners.spotlight: Sent off 11-4 and shaped well when a debut fourth of nine in novice at Nottingham (5f, good to soft) 16 days ago, running on late under hand riding; this son of Starman is open to a fair bit of progress.
runners.comment: Shaped well with Nottingham debut fourth, running on late; open to a fair bit of progress

runners.name: Wolf's Gallop
runners.spotlight: Sergei Prokofiev colt; took a big step forward from his debut with third of nine (Nascent Star and Blondie Larry just behind) in 5f Nottingham novice 16 days ago; may do better still.
runners.comment: Big step forward with third in 5f Nottingham novice 16 days ago; may do better still

runners.name: Drum Major
runners.spotlight: Cheekpieces fitted and posted his best effort when sixth of seven in 5f Wolverhampton novice last month; needs another step forward here, though.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces on and best effort when sixth at Wolverhampton; needs another step forward

runners.name: Seathegulls
runners.spotlight: Encouraging debut seventh in the Brocklesby at Doncaster in March; since gelded and beat one in five-runner Goodwood maiden (5f) eight days ago; more is required.
runners.comment: Debut seventh in Brocklesby; since gelded and beat one at Goodwood; more is required

runners.name: Blondie Larry
runners.spotlight: 9-1, encouraging start with fifth of nine in novice at Nottingham (5f, good to soft) 16 days ago; this son of Showcasing seems sure to build on it.
runners.comment: Encouraging start with fifth at Nottingham; Showcasing colt seems sure to build on it

runners.name: Trust Yourself
runners.spotlight: Son of Showcasing who has beaten one rival in a pair of 6f novices at Ascot and Nottingham this summer; needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: Beaten one rival in a pair of 6f novices at Ascot and Nottingham this summer

races.race_name: Sri Lanka The Wonder Of Asia Handicap

runners.name: Alasrae
runners.spotlight: In fine form since joining George Boughey, gaining a third AW win of 2026 at Lingfield (6f) in April; posted another good effort with third of 11 (a place behind Marcellinus) over C&D 12 days ago; must enter calculations.
runners.comment: A three-time AW winner; good effort with third over C&D latest so must enter calculations

runners.name: Combustion
runners.spotlight: Improving type who completed a 6f AW hat-trick at Lingfield this winter; just his second run on turf when last of 17 in 5f Ascot handicap last month, but poorly drawn there and not out of things.
runners.comment: Completed AW hat-trick this winter; last of 17 at Ascot last month but poorly drawn there

runners.name: Star Chorus
runners.spotlight: A two-time 5f/6f scorer at Southwell this winter; yet to hit top form after a three-month absence, though he was poorly drawn when 15th in 5f Epsom handicap last time; no forlorn hope in a refitted hood.
runners.comment: Poorly drawn when 15th in 5f Epsom handicap last time; no forlorn hope in a refitted hood

runners.name: Supreme King
runners.spotlight: Gained a third win of the season in 6f Salisbury handicap 18 days ago, just holding on; this reliable sort can make his presence felt again nudged up 2lb.
runners.comment: Gained third win of season in 6f Salisbury h'cap 18 days ago; can make his presence felt

runners.name: Angel Of Anfield
runners.spotlight: A fair C&D winner at 2yrs; came in last of ten in 7f Ascot handicap on his seasonal return but this Harry Angel gelding can take a step forward now back in trip.
runners.comment: C&D winner; last of ten on return at Ascot but can take a step forward now back in trip

runners.name: Marcellinus
runners.spotlight: Upwardly-mobile Showcasing gelding; made a winning start in handicaps at Bath (5.5f) in April and has advanced his form with excellent second placings at Hamilton (behind Red Spells Danger) and over C&D (beaten a nose) since; up 3lb but merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Won at Bath before excellent seconds at Hamilton and C&D; merits serious consideration

runners.name: Kiniro
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with 6f success at Lingfield in October for Harry Charlton; comes here in good nick for his new yard, finishing runner-up in 6f Newbury handicap 16 days ago; one to consider.
runners.comment: In good nick for new yard, runner-up in 6f Newbury h'cap 16 days ago; one to consider

runners.name: An Outlaw's Grace
runners.spotlight: Without a win since his debut in June 2024; posted a good C&D second in May but beat only one in 6f York handicap since; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Good C&D second in May but beat only one in 6f York handicap since; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Jax Edge
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer who got back to winning ways when making all in 5.5f handicap at Bath 22 days ago; has a career-high mark to overcome now but can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: C&D scorer; back to winning ways at Bath latest; not ruled out despite a career-high mark

runners.name: Desert Cop
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence and tried in cheekpieces when beating just two in 5f handicap here five days ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: On a long losing sequence and tried in cheekpieces when beating two here five days ago

races.race_name: Sporting Times Sri Lanka Handicap

runners.name: Angel Numbers
runners.spotlight: Winless since her debut but she comes here in decent nick, not best drawn when seventh of 13 in 5f York handicap last time; not out of things off a 3lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Winless since her debut but she comes here in decent nick; not out of things

runners.name: Quantum Power
runners.spotlight: Built on earlier promise this season with a comfortable victory in 5f Newmarket handicap a week ago; up 5lb but another bold showing is on the cards with Billy Loughnane now up.
runners.comment: Comfortable winner at Newmarket a week ago; up 5lb but a bold showing is on the cards

runners.name: Eightthreeone
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start in handicaps at Southwell (6f) in April and has continued in good form, second of nine at Goodwood (5f) last month; ought to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Good second of nine at Goodwood (5f) last month; ought to be thereabouts

runners.name: Dark Alley
runners.spotlight: A fair 5.5f winner at 2yrs; resumed from seven months off with a good fifth of nine in 5f Goodwood handicap last month; needs considering eased 2lb.
runners.comment: Good fifth of nine in 5f Goodwood handicap last month; needs considering eased 2lb

runners.name: Adalida
runners.spotlight: Keen-going sort who proved suited by dropping to 5f when gaining a first win at Lingfield in May; not best drawn when 11th in 5f York handicap last time; can make her presence felt.
runners.comment: First win at Lingfield; not best drawn when 11th at York since; can make presence felt

runners.name: Control Room
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with 5f Catterick maiden victory and signed off for Robert Cowell with a very good second of nine in 5.5f Bath handicap last month; up 3lb but very much one to consider for her new yard.
runners.comment: Signed off for Robert Cowell with very good Bath second; very much one to consider

runners.name: Alkuwarrior
runners.spotlight: Came in last on his first three starts for current yard but revitalised by cheekpieces when bagging 5f Ffos Las handicap 23 days ago; needs headgear to work the oracle for a second time off 7lb higher here.
runners.comment: Revitalised by cheekpieces when scoring at Ffos Las; needs headgear to work oracle again

races.race_name: Sporting Times Sri Lanka British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Ruler Of Time
runners.spotlight: Not This Time gelding; fairly useful form shown when runner-up on four of his five starts, on latest back from four months off in 1m Doncaster novice 22 days ago; the form pick.
runners.comment: Fairly useful form shown when runner-up on four of his five starts; the form pick

runners.name: Gonna Fly
runners.spotlight: Starspangledbanner colt who looked a good prospect when making a winning debut in 1m Newbury novice in October; 20-1, last of eight in Greenham Stakes there on his return; remains with plenty of potential and well worth another chance.
runners.comment: Good prospect with Newbury debut win; last in Greenham there since; worth another chance

runners.name: Lunella
runners.spotlight: 28,000euros foal, 125,000gns yearling; half-sister to four winners, notably Stratum (1m2f-2m5f Flat, RPR 115; useful 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase); dam 1m winner (RPR 78); this Lope Y Fernandez filly appeals on paper but this is no easy debut; hooded.
runners.comment: This Lope Y Fernandez filly appeals on paper but this is no easy debut

runners.name: Peace And Quiet
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced 5yo; has gone very keenly when never dangerous in a pair of 1m novices at Nottingham and here this summer; significantly more is required.
runners.comment: Never dangerous in a pair of 1m novices at Nottingham and here this summer

races.race_name: Sri Lanka Paradise Island Handicap

runners.name: Fireblade
runners.spotlight: Brother to the top-class Cracksman; made good strides for Dylan Cunha last season (scored twice at up to 2m) and he shaped encouragingly over an inadequate 1m2f when fourth of eight in handicap at Pontefract in April; appeals as the sort to do well in 2026 and interesting now back up in trip.
runners.comment: Encouraging fourth over 1m2f at Pontefract in April; interesting now back up in trip
runners.owner: Elb Bloodstock Ltd And R L Banks

runners.name: Claymore
runners.spotlight: A two-time 1m4f scorer in 2025; returned from a spell in Meydan with a solid fifth of 12 in 1m3f handicap at Carlisle four weeks ago; not out of things with Billy Loughnane now up.
runners.comment: A two-time 1m4f scorer in 2025; solid fifth at Carlisle four weeks ago; not out of things

runners.name: Pride Of Donegal
runners.spotlight: A two-time 1m4f scorer at Wolverhampton in the spring; rare poor effort when last of eight in 1m4f Newmarket handicap last time; the sort to bounce back.
runners.comment: Rare poor effort when last in Newmarket handicap last time; the sort to bounce back

runners.name: Spioradalta
runners.spotlight: Most likeable front runner who bagged a second win of 2026 in 1m2f Ripon handicap in May; unable to lead from a poor draw when 11th at York (1m2f) 15 days ago and remains very much one to consider.
runners.comment: Unable to lead from poor draw when 11th at York latest; very much one to consider

runners.name: Wisper
runners.spotlight: Took this in 2025 and she made a winning return over C&D last month; up 3lb but she's a likely player once more with yard going very well.
runners.comment: Took this in 2025 and made a winning return over C&D last month; up 3lb but likely player

runners.name: King's Code
runners.spotlight: Kickstarted 2025 with 1m2f handicap success at Newcastle in January, but he's failed to beat a rival on his last three runs, finishing last of 16 in 1m2f Epsom handicap 22 days ago.
runners.comment: Scored at Newcastle in January but he's failed to beat a rival on his last three runs

runners.name: Majestic
runners.spotlight: Winless since successful in Bahrain (1m2f) in January 2025, but he got back on track with fourth of seven in 1m3f handicap at Hamilton last month; this veteran is no forlorn hope off a 2lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Back on track with fourth at Hamilton; this veteran is no forlorn hope off 2lb lower mark

races.race_name: Visit Sri Lanka Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Glenna
runners.spotlight: Reliable Gleneagles filly who came clear of the rest when a very good second of seven in 1m4f Newbury handicap 16 days ago; big player off the same mark in her bid for a breakthrough victory.
runners.comment: Very good second in 1m4f Newbury handicap 16 days ago; big player off the same mark

runners.name: Kindly Queen
runners.spotlight: Nathaniel filly; in the frame on her first five starts, blinkered when a good second over C&D in May; only fifth of seven in 1m4f Newbury handicap last time but goes in different headgear here and no surprise to see her bounce back.
runners.comment: Only fifth at Newbury last time but no surprise to see her bounce back in headgear change

runners.name: Mohaab
runners.spotlight: Gained a first success at the tenth attempt at Nottingham (1m2f) in May and posted a solid fourth of ten (off a 7lb higher mark) over 1m2f here 26 days ago; not out of things stepping up in trip.
runners.comment: Won at Nottingham; solid fourth here 26 days ago; not out of things stepping up in trip

runners.name: Ticker Tape
runners.spotlight: Time Test filly who is a half-sister to the stable's smart Gold Cup winner Courage Mon Ami; showed promise when in the frame (up to 1m2f) at Wolverhampton and Lingfield on her first two starts but failed to build on that when a free-going fifth of six in 1m4f novice at Newcastle last month; remains with potential though now going into handicaps for top yard.
runners.comment: Failed to build on earlier promise when fifth at Newcastle latest; remains with potential

runners.name: Hot Silk
runners.spotlight: Too Darn Hot filly; has been brought along steadily, a patiently ridden fourth of six in 9.5f Wolverhampton novice last month; appeals as a likely improver now going handicapping stepped up in trip.
runners.comment: Patiently ridden fourth at Wolverhampton latest; likely improver now h'capping up in trip

races.race_name: Truelove Trophy Handicap

runners.name: Bownder
runners.spotlight: Wildly inconsistent this year; better from the front in a small field last time and the return to a switchback track, back up in trip, looks in his favour (fair record on the AW here); whether he reproduces the effort is the question.
runners.comment: Wildly inconsistent this year and whether he reproduces his latest effort is the question

runners.name: Sansanetti
runners.spotlight: Progressive once handicapping over this trip last year, winning four from six (three on Polytrack), latterly off a 3lb lower mark; held in two runs back but this is easier and she ought to fare better.
runners.comment: Progressive handicapping over this trip last year; ought to step up on this year's runs

runners.name: Cuban Girl
runners.spotlight: Often pulled hard, including in the hood, and she'll have to settle better going beyond 1m for the first time after a short break; best effort for this yard was last time, though, despite again edging left (winner progressive), and she holds claims on that.
runners.comment: Claims on latest run but she needs to settle better going beyond 1m for the first time

runners.name: Baloo's Blues
runners.spotlight: Missed the break more often than not and has still to prove his stamina over this far, having run below market expectations when last seen three months ago; questions to answer but 5lb claimer Harry Vigors has been among the winners of late.
runners.comment: Missed the break more often than not and has still to prove his stamina over this far

runners.name: Revelio
runners.spotlight: Underwent wind surgery after one run for Richard Hannon last spring; not without support on the first of his two runs back from a year off and now starts handicap life at a lowly level, stepping back up from 1m under leading apprentice Jack Callan; this task is more realistic.
runners.comment: Starts handicap life at a lowly level, stepping back up from 1m; this is more realistic

races.race_name: Chris, Aleah And Ellanna Family Day Out Handicap

runners.name: Mayaada
runners.spotlight: Said to have run flat at Chester before over-racing and not seeing out 1m4f last time; it's likely this mark is within range but she had plenty of racing last year and has again this term, and she's vulnerable to improvers now.
runners.comment: Likely this mark within range but she's had plenty of racing; vulnerable to improvers now

runners.name: Suddenly I See
runners.spotlight: Made all off 6lb lower round Musselburgh in April; has not been able to lead on either run since (fluffed the start at Beverley; said to have stopped quickly at Sandown a month ago) and has since been gelded; too soon to write him off.
runners.comment: Not able to lead on last two outings and has since been gelded; too soon to write him off

runners.name: Magical Merlot
runners.spotlight: Often pulled hard last year and was gelded over the winter; this season his issue has been blowing the start and that's what cost him any chance last time; up to winning off this mark but has to be considered risky now.
runners.comment: Blowing the start of late; up to winning off this mark but has to be considered risky now

runners.name: Savvy Disko
runners.spotlight: From a speedy family; improved upon last backend's two Newmarket efforts when coming away with a long odds-on favourite to win on his Polytrack comeback here (1m) last month; taken out since on account of unsuitable (good) ground; remains open to improvement now handicapping.
runners.comment: Came away with a long odds-on shot to win AW comeback here; remains open to improvement

runners.name: Knight Of Glory
runners.spotlight: Polytrack maiden win here last winter, when he had them nicely strung out, has been well advertised since; got upset in two runs on dirt at Meydan early in the year and has since been gelded; this task is more realistic in a hood for a yard bang among the winners.
runners.comment: Has been gelded; this race is more realistic, in a hood for a yard bang among the winners

races.race_name: Fabric First Past The Post Handicap

runners.name: Jack Langley
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner here for Simon Dow last year who made a solid start for his new yard back on turf, having been picked up for 11,000gns, at Ripon nine days ago; just cracked late there on his first run in three months and needs considering.
runners.comment: Decent start for his new yard back on turf at Ripon nine days ago; holds solid claims

runners.name: Captain Brett
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-3 at Yarmouth (0-11 elsewhere, often at short prices) when beating a long odds-on shot in a three-runner race nine days ago without the usual hood; fair chance of following up off a 4lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Won a three-runner race nine days ago; fair chance of following up off a 4lb higher mark

runners.name: Kotari
runners.spotlight: Useful a couple of years ago but has regressed sharply and been very expensive to follow in recent times, in various forms of headgear (also had wind surgery); not beaten far last time, though, and has been found a weak race down in class.
runners.comment: Regressed sharply and has been very expensive to follow; not beaten far last time though

runners.name: Stage Show
runners.spotlight: 66-1 winner on his first Flat start since 2024 when making most to win off 2lb lower in a small field at Salisbury last month; back to his inconsistent self when tailed off at Chepstow next time and it will likely depend on what sort of mood he's in.
runners.comment: Back to his inconsistent self when tailed off at Chepstow; it depends what mood he's in

runners.name: Trooper
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner in Belgium/Germany (one in blinkers) who's struggled in five runs for this yard (four over hurdles); the first-time cheekpieces he sported when beaten a long way at Goodwood three weeks ago are replaced.
runners.comment: Struggled in five runs for this yard (four over hurdles); beaten a long way at Goodwood

races.race_name: Choice Training Handicap

runners.name: Ancient State
runners.spotlight: Three previous wins had come on the AW (5f) but he was a revelation at Newmarket eight days ago when hammering a big field over 6f; that may well have been down to the first-time eyeshield and he now has to back it up off an 8lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Easy win in first-time eyeshield eight days ago; has to back it up off an 8lb higher mark

runners.name: Rogue Bullet
runners.spotlight: Yet another to win first time out for this yard when turning in a career-best effort at Brighton last Sunday; makes appeal under a penalty for that, especially with Jack Callan taking off 3lb, and he looks to have Ancient State to worry about.
runners.comment: Career best last Sunday; makes appeal under a penalty, with Jack Callan taking off 3lb

runners.name: Sam's Xpress
runners.spotlight: Four wins on the AW since finishing second in a classified at Yarmouth last summer (0-10 on turf) and he is 26lb higher than that day; would be a surprise winner back from a few months off.
runners.comment: 26lb higher than for last turf run; would be a surprise winner back from a few months off

runners.name: Kiss And Run
runners.spotlight: Arrives in good form, going close at Windsor before anticipating the start last time; losing run is 18, however, and never won off a mark this high.
runners.comment: Arrives in form; losing run is up to 18, however, and she's never won off a mark this high

runners.name: Lazzar
runners.spotlight: His four wins have all come over 6f on the AW and he's 0-10 on turf; having a rare start over 5f tonight and will need to improve on recent efforts.
runners.comment: Having a rare start over 5f tonight and will need to improve on recent efforts

races.race_name: Getaway To Cyprus EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Terminology
runners.spotlight: 360,000gns breeze-up purchase who made a satisfactory debut behind subsequent Queen Mary (Group 2) runner-up Senorita Bonita at Nottingham (5f, good) early in the month; just about sets the standard on that and is entitled to improve.
runners.comment: 360,000gns breeze-up purchase; Nottingham third on debut and she is entitled to improve

runners.name: Najmet Minzaal
runners.spotlight: 50,000euros yearling whose debut fourth on Tapeta six weeks ago (33-1) has been boosted since by those ahead of her; got chopped off early that night before sticking on, and is one to consider.
runners.comment: Debut Tapeta fourth six weeks ago (33-1) has since been boosted by those ahead; considered

runners.name: Betty Boop
runners.spotlight: 75,000gns yearling who's the first foal of Shandra, who won on her 2yo debut (7f) for Dermot Weld; no surprise were she to want more of a test in due course for a trainer who does much better with 2yos on the AW than turf; market will be the best guide.
runners.comment: No surprise were she to want more of a test in due course; market will be the best guide

runners.name: Persian Sunset
runners.spotlight: Useful pedigree but easy to back on two runs last month and finished well held; trainer's strike-rate with juveniles is up this season but it may be in nurseries that she makes her mark.
runners.comment: Finished well held both starts; it may be in nurseries that she makes her mark

runners.name: Penny Capri
runners.spotlight: Showed plenty of toe in her two runs (good/soft), both in the hood; inclined to hang left each time, though, and she'll need to improve; nurseries may be her time to shine.
runners.comment: Inclined to hang left both starts and needs to improve; nurseries may be her time to shine

runners.name: Havana Flash
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns breeze-up 2yo whose dam won three Listed sprints in Ireland; in at the deep end on her 6f Epsom debut (sole newcomer) when shaping with a bit of promise, and entitled to improve for last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: In at the deep end on her 6f Epsom debut; entitled to improve for last year's winning yard

races.race_name: Animus Associates Classified Stakes

runners.name: Desert Charm
runners.spotlight: Arrived following a win when a below-par fourth in this race last summer; always travelling well when winning at Chepstow 12 days ago, however, and she sneaks in here on the ceiling mark having been put up 2lb; player from a handy draw.
runners.comment: Sneaks in here on the ceiling mark having been put up 2lb; player from a handy draw

runners.name: Nicely Curved
