runners.spotlight: Maiden (0-9 in the US) who's been patchy for her current yard (in season when well beaten at a short price on the AW here in February before not taking to hurdles); gave hope when an unlucky-in-running third behind Heer's Sadie last time, however; player.
runners.comment: Gave hope when an unlucky-in-running third behind Heer's Sadie last time; player

runners.name: Rovinia
runners.spotlight: Consistent last year but has lost her way since over a variety of trips; this is her first crack at 0-50 company but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Consistent last year but has lost her way since over a variety of trips; others stronger

runners.name: Accommodation
runners.spotlight: Ran only once for William Haggas (poorly) and has achieved little for her current yard; first-time headgear needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Achieved little for two yards; first-time headgear needs to make a big difference

runners.name: Drafted
runners.spotlight: Trainer won with three of his last five runners coming into today, all three doing it easily, two at big prices; this triple AW winner has shown enough since returned to turf to suggest he can be a factor at this level.
runners.comment: Has shown enough since returned to turf to suggest he can be a factor at this level

runners.name: Blossom In The Air
runners.spotlight: Again didn't convince on the stamina front over 7f (soft) last time and she's drawn wide going up in trip again; bit to find coming out of handicap company.
runners.comment: Drawn wide going up in trip again; bit to find coming out of handicap company

runners.name: Mr Jeffrey
runners.spotlight: Huge prices for all three runs, finishing well beaten, and it's hard to find any positives from stall 1.
runners.comment: Huge prices for all three runs and it's hard to find any positives from stall 1

runners.name: Lowestoft
runners.spotlight: Bit more hope in his two starts in the tongue-tie for his new yard; didn't appear to quite see out 1m on soft ground behind Heer's Sadie and Nicely Curved last time and he's one of the more interesting runners.
runners.comment: Bit more hope in his two starts in the tongue-tie for his new yard; each-way shout

runners.name: Dontwaryboutathing
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in three runs over 6f; this is more realistic in a first-time tongue-tie but seeing some market interest would be wanted before she's considered.
runners.comment: This is more realistic; seeing market interest would be wanted before she's considered

races.race_name: Harris & Bailey Builders Merchants 'Confined' Apprentice Handicap (Which Have Not Won In 2026)

runners.name: Orange Emperor
runners.spotlight: Arron D'Arcy (1-10 overall) rode a winner for the trainer, who won this race two years ago, early in the month; no problem with 7f under him last time but the drop back in trip, minus the usual hood, should be fine; player.
runners.comment: No problem with 7f latest but the drop back in trip minus the hood should be fine; player

runners.name: My Dad Tom
runners.spotlight: Perhaps the return to turf will prove the answer but he hasn't shone in two runs in cheekpieces this year, having been gelded, and is regressive; others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Has not shone in 2026 cheekpieces, having been gelded, and is regressive; others stronger

runners.name: Blindfold Games
runners.spotlight: Excuses since returned to turf, and having Jack Callan up is an obvious plus, but she's an exposed maiden whose best efforts have come on AW; the cheekpieces experiment has quickly been binned and she needs to find more from somewhere.
runners.comment: Excuses since returned to turf and having Jack Callan up is an obvious plus, but exposed

runners.name: Savannah Smiles
runners.spotlight: Well suited by undulating tracks and has come back to form since undergoing wind surgery; now 3lb below her last winning mark and Harry Vigors has plenty of experience for 5lb in this company;  down in class and it's hard to find any negatives.
runners.comment: Well suited by undulating tracks and has come back to form since a wind op; shortlisted

races.race_name: UB40 Ali Campbell At Chepstow Racecourse 24th July Handicap

runners.name: A Daughters Love
runners.spotlight: 10lb 'wrong' when she showed her first form to finish third at Bath this month and she backed that up when third again in Ffos Las classified (also 1m2f, good) on Tuesday; this is a quick turnaround but she's the key player.
runners.comment: Backed up first piece of form when again third at Ffos Las on Tuesday; the key player

runners.name: Lady Lauren
runners.spotlight: Had bits and pieces of form last year but this term has been a struggle and she was tailed off on AW (1m4f) 11 days ago; return to this trip might help but that's not a strong argument.
runners.comment: Had bits and pieces of form last year but this term has been a struggle

runners.name: No Drama Mama
runners.spotlight: Handicap debut at Kempton (1m, AW) wasn't devoid of promise in February but she hasn't built on it; can probably be excused her latest effort when she blew the start over C&D (first-time cheekpieces) last month but her profile is not convincing.
runners.comment: Can probably be excused latest run (blew the start) but her profile is not convincing

runners.name: Summer Oasis
runners.spotlight: Patchy form in her qualifying runs and she's failed to make an impact in two handicaps, latterly when beaten 21l at Windsor (1m, good to soft) 19 days ago; new trip and/or cheekpieces will need to spark some improvement.
runners.comment: No impact in handicaps and new trip and/or cheekpieces will need to spark some improvement

runners.name: Hello Garda
runners.spotlight: Irish raider who's making his second British start after finishing fourth, beaten 12l, in 1m4f Brighton classified (good to firm) on Monday; drops in trip with cheekpieces added and this won't take much winning.
runners.comment: Fourth in 1m4f classified on Monday; drops in trip with cheekpieces added; a possible

runners.name: Madame Passant
runners.spotlight: Didn't shine in a novice and two maidens over 1m, including when 400-1 and trailing home a distant last at Windsor six weeks ago; this handicap debut is her first realistic opportunity so interesting to see how she goes in the market now upped in trip.
runners.comment: Distant last at Windsor six weeks ago but this h'cap debut is her first realistic chance

runners.name: Notable Charm
runners.spotlight: Modest form in a novice and two maidens, most recently when beaten 24l at Salisbury (1m2f, soft) 18 days ago; starts life in handicaps at a realistic level and any market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: Beaten 24l 18 days ago but starts life in handicaps at a realistic level; worth a look

races.race_name: Hayleys Race To Happily Ever After Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Dream On Kent
runners.spotlight: Both runs at Chester; faced no easy task on 6f debut (good) and then ran second in a 7f seller (good to soft); showed speed that day so dropping back to 6f is no big deal; has form claims and he's now visored.
runners.comment: Good form claims on latest Chester second for all that was a seller; now visored

runners.name: Pageant Girl
runners.spotlight: Benefited from the rail when winning at Ripon (6f, good) and it was always going to be tough under a penalty at Newmarket, especially back at 5f; in calmer waters here, Ashley Lewis's claim eases the extra weight and cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: In calmer waters here, Ashley Lewis's claim eases the extra weight and cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Em's Dilemma
runners.spotlight: 28-1 when a tailed-off last in a Chester 7f seller, finishing behind two of these.
runners.comment: 28-1 when a tailed-off last in a Chester seller, finishing behind two of these

runners.name: Lushill
runners.spotlight: Only ran on Tuesday when beaten just over 2l at Newbury (6f, good to firm), outperforming her odds of 100-1; one formline in that race gives her the beating of Miss Moneypit, so she's a definite contender.
runners.comment: Only ran on Tuesday when beaten just over 2l at Newbury; that brings her into this

runners.name: Miss Moneypit
runners.spotlight: Consistent rather than progressive but her middle run was at Ascot (5f, good) and it wasn't a bad race at all last time at Chepstow, where she encountered soft ground for the first time; if seeing out this extra furlong she shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Seems consistent enough and if seeing out this extra furlong she shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Invicta Rose
runners.spotlight: Well held in both her races, though ran on soft ground first time out (6f) and a wide draw did for her at Chester (7f, good to soft); unexposed under these conditions and might have a part to play.
runners.comment: Unexposed under these conditions and might have a part to play

runners.name: Curious Minx
runners.spotlight: Weak 16-1 at Windsor (5f, good to firm) and never really counted, going down by 7l; market can guide again, though.
runners.comment: Weak 16-1 at Windsor (5f, good to firm) and never really counted, going down by 7l

runners.name: Super Tuscan
runners.spotlight: Initial efforts nothing to shout about, though he did show some early speed and has been gelded; may fare better.
runners.comment: Has shown some early speed and has been gelded, so may fare better now

races.race_name: Bell Cleaning Handicap

runners.name: Corniche Girl
runners.spotlight: String of consistent efforts since her AW win last summer, including from a wide stall back on turf six weeks ago; no obvious reason she won't give her running again in a race in which a few have something to prove.
runners.comment: String of consistent efforts since her AW win last summer; set to go well once more
runners.owner: C Munro, Mrs M Gander And Partner

runners.name: Hallandale Beach
runners.spotlight: Run to a similar level in two starts in the tongue-tie since handicapping; latest effort over 5f can be marked up a touch (stumbled at the start and came widest from the outside stall at Windsor) and he holds decent claims.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; latest effort over 5f can be marked up a touch and he holds decent claims

runners.name: Ken Brulee
runners.spotlight: Made all from the rail draw when winning at 33-1 off 5lb lower at Lingfield last month (first-time cheekpieces; switches to a visor tonight); since held twice at short prices and this is stronger up from 0-55 company.
runners.comment: Held twice at short prices since 33-1 shock and this is stronger up from 0-55 company

runners.name: Luna Beaux
runners.spotlight: Dual Wolverhampton winner earlier in the year, latterly off a 2lb higher mark, who found the race not panning out her way under a hold-up ride last time; having the vastly experience Taryn Langley take off 5lb won't hurt and she's one to consider.
runners.comment: Found the race not panning out her way under a hold-up ride last time; shortlisted

runners.name: Who Is Alice
runners.spotlight: Won her maiden here last summer (7f) and this is the easiest race she's run in since handicapping; two poor efforts last month will need completely overlooking however.
runners.comment: This is easier but two poor efforts last month will need completely overlooking

runners.name: Pimentel
runners.spotlight: Wolverhampton winner for Ashley Lewis last autumn off 3lb lower; runs in a sprint for the first time since handicapping and would want to see a bit of market strength back from almost six months off.
runners.comment: Drops in trip; would want to see a bit of market strength back from almost six months off

races.race_name: Paving Fields Construction Maiden Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Creciente
runners.spotlight: Has shown bits of form and was beaten less than 3l in a better contest at Salisbury (7f, good) 13 days ago; her mark continues to fall and this looks to be her best opportunity to date.
runners.comment: Has shown bits of form and was beaten less than 3l at Salisbury 13 days ago.

runners.name: Okiru
runners.spotlight: Has been placed three times on AW but not so good in two starts on turf; his mark continues to drop and first-time blinkers replace cheekpieces; needs to show more.
runners.comment: Has been placed three times on AW but not so good in two starts on turf.

runners.name: Jackabi
runners.spotlight: A fair 7f juvenile maiden but he tends to race freely and did well considering, when beaten 4l at Chester (7.5f, soft) 15 days ago, in first-time tongue-tie to go with hood (retained); takes a drop in grade and may be interesting if let loose in front.
runners.comment: Tends to race freely and did well considering when beaten 4l at Chester 15 days ago.

runners.name: Mister McGregor
runners.spotlight: 0-25 but has been runner-up five times, the last twice in classified events at Wolverhampton AW this year where his best chance of success lies; continues in new headgear combination but vulnerable to less-exposed sorts.
runners.comment: 0-25 but has been runner-up five times, the last twice in classifieds at Wolverhampton.

runners.name: Thai Princess
runners.spotlight: Has shown only modest form on three runs on AW; has had wind surgery since last start in April and makes turf/handicap debut; no surprise were she to show improvement for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Has shown only modest form on three runs on AW; makes turf/handicap debut.

runners.name: Havana's Dream
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in all 12 starts and she's been tailed off in three runs this year; cheekpieces back on replacing visor; others preferred.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all 12 starts and she's been tailed off in three runs this year.

runners.name: Imagine That
runners.spotlight: Hasn't shown much in four starts, including on handicap debut when fifth of nine at Goodwood (6f, soft) 15 days ago; steps up in trip and needs to show improvement.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much in four starts, including on handicap debut when fifth at Goodwood.

races.race_name: Congratulations Dan & Grace Handicap

runners.name: Great Blasket
runners.spotlight: Has won seven of his 25 races on turf; has run well in three starts since returning last month and should run his usual solid race.
runners.comment: Won seven of his 25 races on turf; has run well in three runs since returning to action.

runners.name: Rating
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on turf at the 20th attempt when accounting for three rivals at Bath (1m, firm) last month; below par when fifth of 13 at Salisbury (1m, good) 13 days ago; win record is offputting.
runners.comment: Off the mark on turf at the 20th attempt when beating three rivals at Bath last month.

runners.name: Gennadius
runners.spotlight: 1-21 but ran his best race for a while when runner-up at Pontefract (1m, good) last Sunday; claims if he can back that up.
runners.comment: Ran his best race for a while when runner-up at Pontefract last Sunday.

runners.name: Spirit Of The Bay
runners.spotlight: Two of her four wins have came at this track, one over today's distance; fourth in both starts this year but may be vulnerable to younger rivals.
runners.comment: Fourth in both starts this year but may be vulnerable to younger rivals.

runners.name: Happy Banner
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in an AW juvenile novice in December 2024; has ran well to be third on both starts this year at Bath and Salisbury; this also represents a class drop and he shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Has ran well to be third on both starts this year at Bath and Salisbury.

runners.name: Stardancer
runners.spotlight: Promise in AW novices and on handicap debut when third at Doncaster (7f, good) last June; has since had wind surgery and should be sharper following his return from ten-month layoff when sixth of seven at Nottingham (8.5f, good) last month in first-time tongue-tie (retained); another to consider.
runners.comment: Should be sharper following his return from layoff when sixth at Nottingham last month.

runners.name: Echalar
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced on turf; out of the frame in six starts since winning at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in January; takes a drop in grade and as low in the weights as he's ever been; not out of this.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in six starts since winning at Wolverhampton in January.

races.race_name: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap

runners.name: Some Nightmare
runners.spotlight: Six of his eight wins have been at this track, the last three over today's distance; must come into calculations.
runners.comment: Six of his eight wins have been at this track, the last three over today's distance.
runners.owner: Mrs S M Maine & Roger Gould & Partner

runners.name: Dragon God
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but finished third twice in the spring; drops back in trip and still has time on his side; should not be far away if he shows his best form.
runners.comment: 0-11 but finished third twice in the spring; drops back in trip.

runners.name: Macarone
runners.spotlight: Sole success on turf came in 2023 when trained by Craig Lidster; has win three times since on AW and left Ivan Furtado last month; unplaced in three runs for current yard and doesn't look ready to capitalise on career-low mark.
runners.comment: Has four career wins but unplaced in three runs for current yard.

runners.name: Secret Handsheikh
runners.spotlight: Has eight wins on turf but losing run is mounting up; needs to rediscover his form.
runners.comment: Has eight wins on turf but losing run is mounting up; needs to rediscover his form.

runners.name: Act Of Violence
runners.spotlight: Two wins came over 6f in 2023 and 2024 when trained by Tim Easterby; no joy in four starts for David Loughnane and steps back to 5f on his stable debut; on a career-low mark and market watch is advised.
runners.comment: No joy in four starts for David Loughnane and steps back to 5f on his stable debut.

runners.name: Captain Bentley
runners.spotlight: Scored at 80-1 to land a classified contest over C&D last July but ran poorly in handicaps subsequently; makes his first appearance of the year but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Scored at 80-1 to land a classified over C&D last July but ran poorly subsequently.

races.race_name: Reecer Group Handicap

runners.name: Sioux Warrior
runners.spotlight: 1-25 but he went close over 7f at Wolverhampton in March and his Bath third last month (5.7f, good to firm) was another sound effort; should remain competitive with first-time cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: 1-25 but he went close at Wolverhampton in March and at Bath when third last month.

runners.name: Autumn Angel
runners.spotlight: Back to form when scoring over C&D (good to soft) last month and has made the frame twice since over C&D; she looks sure to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Back to form when scoring over C&D last month and has made the frame twice since over C&D.

runners.name: Ravenglass
runners.spotlight: Last of five wins came at this track (7f) in May 2025 off this mark; returned to form when beaten a head here (7f, good) 12 days ago but not sure the step back to 6f for the first time since February 2024 will be in his favour.
runners.comment: Returned to form when beaten a head here 12 days ago.

runners.name: Weston Court
runners.spotlight: Has struggled since landing two wins in the summer of 2024; another change of headgear with first-time blinkers taking over from a visor.
runners.comment: Has struggled since landing two wins in the summer of 2024.

runners.name: Marisitta
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden and lacks consistency to boot; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Still a maiden and lacks consistency to boot; hard to fancy.

runners.name: Thanks Dad
runners.spotlight: Two wins came at Southwell AW last year; down the field twice last month back on turf and best to leave until returning to Southwell.
runners.comment: Two wins came at Southwell AW last year; down the field twice last month.

runners.name: Reidh
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 7f for Richard Fahey in 2024; not added to that tally since, and has been below-par in three starts for new yard; tries a new trip and runs with first-time tongue-tie and blinkers which need to have a positive impact.
runners.comment: Won twice over 7f for Richard Fahey in 2024; below-par in three starts for new yard.

races.race_name: Kam Tin River Handicap (Class 5) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.name: Dash
runners.spotlight: Placed for first time when keeping-on third over C&D in March, but two down-the-field finishes have followed (7f/6f) and he's now 0-11.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D in March but two down-the-field runs followed; now 0-11

runners.name: No Other Choice
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden; posted sound effort when third over C&D on turf return a fortnight ago, but is drawn much wider today; swaps hood for first-time blinkers; others preferred.
runners.comment: Sound effort when third over C&D last month but draw much wider today
runners.owner: Cheng Chung Wah & Enzo Cheng Tin Hang

runners.name: Sonic Boom
runners.spotlight: Gave good account when close third at Happy Valley this month and remains on a workable mark, but has modest 1-35 strike-rate; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Ran well when close third at Happy Valley this month but has modest 1-35 strike-rate
runners.owner: George Lee Hing Fai, Wong Che Leung Et Al

runners.name: Taihang Scenery
runners.spotlight: 18-race maiden; made the frame on three of his five starts this year (5f-6f) and didn't run badly when sixth at Happy Valley (5f) last month, but has become somewhat frustrating.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly last month but is 0-18 now and has become somewhat frustrating

runners.name: Lakeshore Hero
runners.spotlight: Placed on dirt here last month and wasn't beaten far when seventh from stall 10 at Happy Valley 24 days ago; drawn even wider here but Zac Purton takes the ride; could have a say if he gets a good pace to aim at.
runners.comment: Drawn wide but could have a say if he gets a strong pace to aim at

runners.name: Multidutch
runners.spotlight: Well beaten over 7f here three weeks ago and has now been unplaced in all 15 Hong Kong starts.
runners.comment: Well beaten over 7f here three weeks ago and has now been unplaced all 15 Hong Kong starts

runners.name: Winning Diamond
runners.spotlight: 0-12 but looked suited by drop back to 6f when fourth at Happy Valley last month and is another 2lb lower here; considered each-way.
runners.comment: Fourth when dropped back to 6f at Happy Valley last month and another 2lb lower here

runners.name: Tai Victory
runners.spotlight: Reserve; struggled on recent stable debut at Happy Valley and is now 0-13 in Hong Kong.
runners.comment: Struggled on recent stable debut at Happy Valley and is now 0-13 in Hong Kong

runners.name: Noble Fans
runners.spotlight: Reserve; placed off much higher mark over C&D on seasonal debut in September, but subsequent form has been underwhelming; needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D on seasonal debut but subsequent form has been underwhelming

runners.name: Autumn Vibes
runners.spotlight: Bettered underwhelming stable debut when very close second at Happy Valley this month and remains well handicapped on some of his Class 4 form from earlier in the season; leading contender.
runners.comment: Went very close at Happy Valley this month and remains well handicapped; good chance

runners.name: Solar River
runners.spotlight: Appreciated ease in grade and ran well from the front when fifth at Happy Valley last month; well drawn to attack here and has good 10lb claimer Nichola Yuen enlisted; can make a bold bid.
runners.comment: Fifth when dropped to this grade last month; well drawn to attack under good 10lb claimer

runners.name: Island Breezes
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over 5f at Happy Valley in December/January, but has been out of form lately and his torrid run of luck draw-wise has continued.
runners.comment: Out of form lately and his torrid run of luck draw-wise has continued

runners.name: Aquaman
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; managed only a never-dangerous seventh over C&D when dropped to this grade a fortnight ago and has another wide draw.
runners.comment: Never-dangerous seventh when dropped to this grade this month; drawn wide again
runners.owner: Steve Chan Chi Fung & Terry Ng Ting Chi

runners.name: Circuit Victory
runners.spotlight: Found things happening too quickly over 5f last month and now returns to a more suitable trip, but this regressive 6yo is having a disappointing season and is poorly drawn today.
runners.comment: Regressive 6yo having a disappointing season; poorly drawn today

runners.name: Heroic Master
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on consecutive appearances at Happy Valley in April/May (6f/5f) and didn't run badly in a Class 4 there this month; swaps usual blinkers for first-time cheekpieces; has a big role to play from stall 1.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly in a Class 4 last time; has big role to play from stall 1

runners.name: Always Fluke
runners.spotlight: Gained his only win in 2023 but has finished second on three of his five starts in this basement grade, the latest a fortnight ago, and has strong each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Without a win since 2023 but has been second on three of five starts in this grade

races.race_name: Lam Tsuen River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.name: Falcon Hunter
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; outran 105-1 odds when placed at Happy Valley in February, but well down the field there on both subsequent outings and has tough draw.
runners.comment: Placed at big odds in February but well down the field on both outings since

runners.name: Glorious Ryder
runners.spotlight: Forced a dead-heat over 1m when dipping into Class 5 in March but soundly beaten back in this higher grade three times since, most recently when dropped back to 6f.
runners.comment: Gained latest win over 1m in Class 5; has struggled since returned to this grade

runners.name: Genius Baby
runners.spotlight: Consistent since win off 5lb lower at Happy Valley in December and made the frame over C&D on last two appearances; may not have the early zip to make full use of his favourable draw but ought to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Consistent since Happy Valley win in December and ought to be on the premises again
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Pang Wai Hak, Frankie Pang Ka Yeung & A Pang Ka Wai

runners.name: Little Monster
runners.spotlight: Held on well to make all from stall 11 over C&D last month, opening his account at the tenth attempt, and did well to finish fifth off new mark three weeks ago, given he was slowly away and made ground on the outside of the field on the home turn; remains of strong interest.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D last month and did well to finish fifth here last time; big player

runners.name: Ka Ying Radiance
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after six starts but, from stall 12, made smooth headway before going very close over C&D last month; Zac Purton keeps the ride; seems likely to strike soon.
runners.comment: Went very close from stall 12 here last month and looks poised to open account soon

runners.name: Synergy Express
runners.spotlight: Yet to show anything more than minor promise after six runs (5f-7f) and almost certainly needs a drop in grade.
runners.comment: Yet to show anything more than minor promise after six runs; needs a drop in grade

runners.name: Hayday
runners.spotlight: Lurks on a dangerous mark (even before Nichola Yuen's 10lb claim is considered), but is yet to win this season and disappointed when well drawn at Happy Valley last month; needs to up his game to overcome stall 12 here.
runners.comment: Lurks on a dangerous mark but disappointed when well drawn last month; in stall 12 today

runners.name: Anode
runners.spotlight: Placed over 7f in early part of this season, but has become disappointing and was well beaten over C&D when dropped to this grade six weeks ago; has the inside stall again but needs a revival.
runners.comment: Well beaten over C&D when dropped to this grade last month; revival needed

runners.name: Ghorgan
runners.spotlight: 0-17 in Hong Kong, having been campaigned mainly over 7f, but was respectable fifth when dropped to this grade a fortnight ago and, given his tendency to race freely, this drop to 6f is worth a go.
runners.comment: Respectable fifth over 7f in this grade a fortnight ago; drop to 6f looks worth a go

runners.name: California Bay
runners.spotlight: Opened Hong Kong account with narrow 6f dirt win last month, but safely held off today's mark twice since (turf/dirt); needs to kick on again.
runners.comment: Twice safely held since dirt win in early May; needs to kick on again

runners.name: Iron Security
runners.spotlight: Managed only ninth from a favourable draw when dropped to this grade at Happy Valley this month and has now been unplaced all five Hong Kong starts; opposable from stall 11.
runners.comment: Managed only ninth from a favourable draw when dropped to this grade this month
runners.owner: Jimmy Tang Kwok Leung, Ho Kai Man & Ricky Lee Wing Ngai

runners.name: Green Angel
runners.spotlight: Reserve; safely held on all three starts (6f-7f) and probably needs more weight off his back.
runners.comment: Safely held on all three starts (6f-7f) and probably needs more weight off his back

runners.name: Turf Quest
runners.spotlight: Struggled to get competitive when a big-priced outsider for recent debut (5f).
runners.comment: Struggled to get competitive when a big-priced outsider for recent debut (5f)
runners.owner: Dr John Shum Ding Ping & Dr Lawrence Chan Pui Wing

runners.name: Copartner A I
runners.spotlight: Out of a dual 6f Group 3 winner in Australia; came second in a recent 5f trial and evidently has ability; makes official debut.
runners.comment: Out of a dual 6f Group 3 winner; second in a recent trial; makes official debut

runners.name: Good Chap
runners.spotlight: Reserve; dual 6f dirt winner this year; placed off today's mark on that surface last month but may not be seen to same effect back on turf.
runners.comment: Has done well on dirt this year but may not be seen to same effect back on turf
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Edmund Hon Hing Cheung

runners.name: Armour War Eagle
runners.spotlight: Placed three times (once over C&D) in Class 3 this year and went close when dropped to this level in April (6f, dirt); can win in this grade soon but has been dealt a duff hand draw-wise.
runners.comment: Can win in this grade soon but has been dealt a duff hand draw-wise

races.race_name: Nam Chung River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.name: Reliable Dad
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; made the frame three consecutive times here (1m-1m1f) in January-March and did not enjoy the run of the race when only seventh here (1m) this month, after a break; tries a new trip today; shortlisted.
runners.comment: In the frame three times here this year; had excuses last time; interesting over new trip
runners.owner: Chris So Chun Hung & Mandy Kwan

runners.name: Star Brose
runners.spotlight: Put a below-par run behind him when fourth in C&D race won by Double Win a fortnight ago, but his only win from 19 starts was gained in lower-grade company; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Ran well when fourth over C&D last month, but others in this line-up look stronger

runners.name: Enthralled
runners.spotlight: Not at best this season but handicapped accordingly now and has been placed twice here (1m2f/1m1f) in recent weeks; ought to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Placed twice here in recent weeks; ought to be right in the thick of things again

runners.name: Noisy Boy
runners.spotlight: Patently not the force of old but, having tumbled down the weights, looked unlucky not to finish closer than fifth over C&D on Sunday; recent 2lb drop enables this dip into Class 4 and good 10lb claimer Nichola Yuen is now enlisted; must be considered.
runners.comment: Unlucky not to finish closer than fifth over C&D on Sunday; eased in grade today
runners.owner: Leung Lun Ping & Ricky Leung Ghin Ming

runners.name: Ocean Impact
runners.spotlight: Made no further progress after breakthrough win at Happy Valley (1m1f; first-time blinkers) in November, but is now 2lb below that mark and his last two runs were respectable (1m3f/1m2f; cheekpieces refitted last time); a possible.
runners.comment: Last two runs were respectable and he's now 2lb below his winning mark; a possible

runners.name: Fast Speed
runners.spotlight: Made low-key stable debut over 1m here this month, but that was his first run for nearly a year and he could still have untapped potential over this longer trip; has possibilities if that recent outing brought him on.
runners.comment: Made low-key stable debut this month but that was his first run for nearly a year
runners.owner: Howard Tai Wai Yin & Yim Hin Keung

runners.name: Joyful Prosperity
runners.spotlight: Placed in this higher grade since narrow win at Happy Valley (1m3f) in March but seems to have gone off the boil lately.
runners.comment: Won in Class 5 in March but seems to have gone off the boil lately
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Silas S S Yang & Michelle H Y Yang

runners.name: On The Lash
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner in Class 5 this season, most recently when allowed to dictate from the front here (1m1f) last month; found things tougher when upped to this grade a fortnight ago, though.
runners.comment: Three Class 5 wins this season; found things tougher in this grade a fortnight ago

runners.name: Massive Glory
runners.spotlight: Made quite promising debut in April (7f) but two disappointing efforts have followed (1m/1m2f); remains best watched.
runners.comment: Made quite promising debut but two disappointing efforts have followed

runners.name: Mission Giant
runners.spotlight: Reserve; belied huge odds when fourth at Happy Valley (1m1f) in April, his second Hong Kong start, but well beaten twice since; dropped in grade today; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Fourth at huge odds in April but well beaten twice since; look elsewhere

runners.name: Super Goldendragon
runners.spotlight: Opened account with shock 88-1 success at Happy Valley (1m3f) in April, his sixth start, and has been placed here (1m1f/1m2f) twice since; may yet have a bigger run in him; respected.
runners.comment: Placed twice here (1m1f/1m2f) since shock win at Happy Valley (1m3f) in April
runners.owner: Yip Shu Bun & Jonathan Chok Shee Feng

runners.name: Romantic Fantasy
runners.spotlight: Just too good for Super Goldendragon here (1m1f) last month, but has questions to answer after two disappointing efforts and might be a bit too high in the weights now anyway.
runners.comment: Two disappointing runs have followed last month's narrow course win (1m1f)

runners.name: Charity Together
runners.spotlight: Pipped Volcanic Spark over C&D in March but has suffered a dip in form since and remains on a tough mark.
runners.comment: Has suffered dip in form since narrow C&D win in March; still on fairly tough mark

runners.name: Volcanic Spark
runners.spotlight: In-form 6yo who led close home to score over 1m1f here lasrt month and was denied a clear run when fifth over C&D four weeks later; holds each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Scored over 1m1f here last month and was denied clear run when fifth over C&D off new mark

runners.name: Serangoon
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won quite stylishly over 1m1f here in February, but has not been at the same level since and is still 4lb above that mark.
runners.comment: Quite stylish course winner (1m1f) in February but not at same level since

runners.name: Double Win
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to fend off Enthralled over C&D last month and has now won three times this season; looks vulnerable after latest 6lb rise, though.
runners.comment: Narrow C&D winner last month; looks vulnerable after latest 6lb rise
runners.owner: Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk & Ma Yuen Yu

races.race_name: NG Tung River Handicap (Class 4) (All Weather)

runners.name: Bull Attitude
runners.spotlight: Disappointing on turf at Happy Valley last month, but made the frame five consecutive times over C&D beforehand and has obvious each-way claims if judged on those efforts.
runners.comment: Well held on turf last month, but in the frame five consecutive times over C&D beforehand

runners.name: King Of Selection
runners.spotlight: Won when trained in New Zealand but has struggled on all seven Hong Kong starts (7f-1m3f); makes stable/dirt debut today; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Has struggled on all seven Hong Kong starts; makes stable/dirt debut today

runners.name: Conrad The Great
runners.spotlight: Safely held on turf when upped to 1m this month and now 0-10, but has twice finished second over 6f on dirt this year and it's possible he'll be suited by this trip on this surface.
runners.comment: Not yet proven over 1m but runner-up twice over 6f on this surface this year
runners.owner: Tam Man Wai, Wilson Tam Man Shu & Dr Tam Po Chor

runners.name: Happy Universe
runners.spotlight: Gained a narrow dirt win over 1m1f here in April and bounced back quickly from a poor run when very close second over that trip three weeks ago; may not be ideally suited by this drop back in trip but still respected in current form.
runners.comment: Won over 1m1f here in April and went very close over that trip three weeks ago; respected

runners.name: Perfect Team
runners.spotlight: Regressive 7yo who has not won since January 2025; on a much-reduced mark now, though, and recent efforts have been respectable; contender if boosted by first-time visor.
runners.comment: On much-reduced mark and recent efforts have been respectable; a possible if visor helps
runners.owner: Ringo Ng Yiu Kwok & Lisa Ng Chan Choi Wan

runners.name: California Star
runners.spotlight: Doubled Hong Kong tally with C&D win off 3lb lower in February but recent efforts have been underwhelming; needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: Doubled HK tally with C&D win in February, but recent efforts have been underwhelming

runners.name: Chill Kaka
runners.spotlight: Won twice over C&D last year and has been placed twice on dirt in recent weeks (1m/1m1f); well drawn and still on a good mark; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Placed twice on dirt in recent weeks; well drawn today and still on a good mark; player

runners.name: Night Purosangue
runners.spotlight: Showed career-best form when beating Chill Kaka by 2l over C&D last month, but a 7lb rise demands even more from him and he didn't fire over 1m1f here three weeks ago.
runners.comment: C&D winner in May but didn't fire last time and remains on quite daunting mark

runners.name: Never Peter Out
runners.spotlight: Gained all four wins over C&D; well beaten over 7f on turf last month but returns to his preferred surface on a good mark and can't be ruled out, despite awkward draw.
runners.comment: Well beaten on turf in May but returns to preferred surface on good mark; can't rule out

runners.name: Fashion Legend
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent this season but made good late headway over 6f here a fortnight ago and ought to be suited by this move back up in trip; on a good mark; impossible to rule out.
runners.comment: Inconsistent this season but suited by this C&D and on a good mark; impossible to rule out
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Boniface Ho Ka Kui

runners.name: Supreme Agility
runners.spotlight: Safely held in two Class 3 races last month (dirt/turf) but has largely been in good form in this grade this season, including for C&D win under Zac Purton in November; back in Class 4 and reunited with the champion jockey today; likely contender.
runners.comment: Safely held in two Class 3s last month but of renewed interest in the grade with Purton up
runners.owner: Rotary Club of Sha Tin Syndicate

runners.name: Foremost Teddy
runners.spotlight: Three Class 5 dirt wins this season, most recently when prevailing by a nose over 1m1f ten weeks ago; drawn much wider today, though, and tends to find things tough in this higher grade.
runners.comment: Narrow Class 5 winner here in April but tends to find things tough at this level
runners.owner: Tse Ming Yee & Emily Tse Wing Man

runners.name: Natural High
runners.spotlight: Reserve; dual 6f dirt winner; back on preferred surface after low-key turf run last month, but has stamina to prove over the new trip.
runners.comment: Dual 6f dirt winner; has stamina to prove over this new trip

runners.name: Sight Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Reserve; dual C&D winner this season; arguably posted career-best performance when close second over 6f on turf here last month, but his latest run over that C&D was disappointing and he's still on a tough mark for this dirt return.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season but returns to dirt on a tough mark

runners.name: Prestige Win
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all four Hong Kong starts; seemed to cope pretty well with this surface when midfield over 6f last month, but has tough draw today and needs this new trip to unlock improvement.
runners.comment: Unplaced all four Hong Kong starts (6f) and has tough draw for first attempt at 1m

runners.name: Leslie
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner this season; well beaten on latest outing ten weeks ago, though, and others in this field look better handicapped.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season but well beaten last time; others look better handicapped

races.race_name: Lam Tsuen River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.name: Navas G
runners.spotlight: All three starts over C&D, finishing a close third on his debut in April but failing to build on that in two starts since; the outside stall won't help him here either.
runners.comment: Close third on his debut over C&D in April, but twice held since; drawn widest

runners.name: Same To You
runners.spotlight: Two of his three wins have come over C&D and he's been running well in defeat lately, but stall 12 won't make things easy.
runners.comment: Has been running well in defeat lately, but stall 12 won't make things easy
runners.owner: Colin Cheung Kwok Lam, Amos Cheung Sai Keung & Gary H K Yau

runners.name: Glaciated
runners.spotlight: Sole success came over C&D in December; patchy form since, but had a poor draw to contend with on a few occasions and he has the inside stall this time; could go well.
runners.comment: Patchy form since winning over C&D in December, but could go well from the inside stall
runners.owner: Alan Chiu Chun Ming, Sandy Cheng Chuk Wah & W Law Chi Yin

runners.name: Enjoy Golf
runners.spotlight: Needed a drop into a Class 5 when making all from a wide draw over C&D last month, though he did run well back at this level when third here last time; drawn high again.
runners.comment: Only recent win came in a Class 5; drawn high again

runners.name: Fortune Supernova
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 0-12 in Hong Kong and change of stables made no difference last time; more needed.
runners.comment: 0-12 in Hong Kong and change of stables made no difference last time

runners.name: Forever Fancy
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in all five starts including two over C&D; may need a drop in grade.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in all five starts including two over C&D; may need a drop in grade
runners.owner: James Lau Chi Wing, Lau Kai Tai & Michelle Lau Wing Sheung

runners.name: Ka Ying Resilience
runners.spotlight: Sole success came over C&D just under a year ago; returns from six months off, but he has won his last two trials so by no means dismissed.
runners.comment: Sole success came over C&D; returns from six months off, but has won his last two trials

runners.name: Yee Cheong Glory
runners.spotlight: No impression over 1m here last time, but both wins have come over C&D and he drops into a Class 4 for the first time since the latest of those in March; respected from his good draw.
runners.comment: Drops to a Class 4 for the first time since winning over C&D in March; respected
runners.owner: Choi Hiu Fung & Choi Yu So Fong

runners.name: Elegant Life
runners.spotlight: Both local wins at Happy Valley (5f/6f) this year, but he has run several good races over this C&D; showed that this sort of mark wasn't beyond him last time and shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Showed that this sort of mark wasn't beyond him last time and shouldn't be far away

runners.name: The Concentration
runners.spotlight: Has won over C&D, but seen more often over 7f these days with his two wins in Class 5 company at the start of the year coming over that trip; opposable at this level from a wide draw.
runners.comment: Seen more often over 7f these days; opposable at this level from a wide draw

runners.name: Francis Meynell
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 0-17 in Hong Hong and he appears to need the extra furlong these days; first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: 0-17 in Hong Hong and appears to need the extra furlong these days; blinkers on
runners.owner: Albert Hung Chao Hong & Kevin Hung Dingteng

runners.name: Unstoppaple
runners.spotlight: Beaten 2l when sixth on his debut over C&D last month, doing all his best work late; interesting if stepping up at all from that.
runners.comment: Beaten 2l when sixth on his debut over C&D last month; interesting if stepping up at all
runners.owner: Lee Chit Ming & David Mok Tai Wai

runners.name: King's Arrow
runners.spotlight: Just a midfield finish on last month's debut here (5f, 46-1), but he was noted making good late headway; worth a second look over this extra furlong.
runners.comment: Midfield finish on last month's debut over 5f here, but was noted making good late headway
runners.owner: Jameson Leung Tai Lin & Rene Leung Yan Ting

runners.name: Phoenix Light
runners.spotlight: All four wins have come over C&D, but it's well over two years since he has got his head in front; better drawn than has mainly been the case lately, but others still look more solid.
runners.comment: All four wins over C&D, but well over two years since he has got his head in front

runners.name: Grouper
runners.spotlight: Went close on his debut over C&D last month, but hasn't built on it in either outing since; Zac Purton takes the ride, but drawn wider than ideal.
runners.comment: Went close on his debut over C&D last month, but hasn't built on it in either outing since

runners.name: Amazing Victory
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over 6f/7f here; still 4lb higher than when winning at Happy Valley a year ago and, although runner-up off this mark there last month, he has become inconsistent.
runners.comment: Still 4lb higher than when winning at Happy Valley a year ago; has become inconsistent

races.race_name: Sha Tau Kok River Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)

runners.name: Master Lucky
runners.spotlight: 0-7 since his winning debut over 5f here, but has been placed in his last two starts over C&D; no reason why he shouldn't go well again.
runners.comment: Placed in his last two starts over C&D; no reason why he shouldn't go well again

runners.name: Star Satyr
runners.spotlight: 1-18 in Hong Kong, the win coming from 12lb higher over C&D just over a year ago; is proving inconsistent, though, and has a bit to find with a couple of these on latest running.
runners.comment: 12lb lower than when winning over C&D just over a year ago, but inconsistent since

runners.name: Solid Car
runners.spotlight: 0-15 including three runs in Australia; has around 3l to find with Master Lucky on latest running over C&D; tongue-tie give a go.
runners.comment: 0-15 including three runs in Australia; behind Master Lucky last time; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Gallant Design
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but placed in his last three starts over C&D; Zac Purton takes over, but he has the outside stall today; swaps blinkers for a visor.
runners.comment: Placed in his last three starts over C&D, but has the outside stall today; visor on

runners.name: Lunar Dash
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far into seventh on his return over 6f here last month, but didn't run very well in one attempt over this far and he has a wide draw.

runners.name: Oak Hill
runners.spotlight: Put in some big strides late this venue over this trip as a market drifter. Usually gets back. Capable of making an impact.
runners.owner: Lindsay Park Bloodstock, R & C Legh Racing(R Legh) Et Al

runners.name: Payline
runners.spotlight: Long time since last saluted. Never a threat after showing no early speed from the barriers here at this trip. Could get into the placings.
runners.owner: Mrs C L Munce, T D Chivers Et Al

runners.name: Yellow Brick
runners.spotlight: The Archer winner Rockhampton 5 May. Settled back and didn't figure in heavy ground in the Stradbroke Hcp this track. At optimum distance. Warrants attention.
runners.owner: Mrs L J Sears, Ms M L Sears & Mrs S E Wee

runners.name: Hidden Wealth
runners.spotlight: Grand campaigner. Led, gave away final stages here at this distance. Leads, handy. Right in this.
runners.owner: Wessel Thoroughbreds, M A McLean Et Al

runners.name: About To Explode
runners.spotlight: Raced midfield, let down powerfully here at this distance. Hard fit and one for exotics.
runners.owner: D Azzopardi, A Bryant Et Al

runners.name: Givemethebeatboys
runners.spotlight: Ranged up but punctured late here. Among the dangers.
runners.owner: Bronsan Racing Syndicate, C Sands & Est T Marnane

runners.name: Hatchet
runners.spotlight: Kept finding the line without threatening in the slosh this track at this distance. Not fancied.
runners.owner: Mrs C E Chapman, A R B Chapman Et Al

runners.name: King Of Sparta
runners.spotlight: Raced in back half and never likely here second-up. Has an adverse draw and up in weight. Needs to turn it around quickly.
runners.owner: Yarraman Park Stud, Grant Bloodstock Pty Ltd Syndicate Et Al

runners.name: King Kapa
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Finished well held at Doomben. Awkwardly drawn. Others with stronger claims.
runners.owner: Strong Bloodstock, B Armstrong Et Al

runners.name: Moravia
runners.spotlight: Resuming (18-weeks). Two pleasing trial hitouts leading into return and is a first-up winner. Has an adverse draw for return. Has some claims fresh in this line-up.
runners.owner: Winkurra Colts, T D Davis Et Al

runners.name: Brudenell
runners.spotlight: Last win was 28 Feb 2025. Just missed at Scone 16 May. Showed a fine turn of foot from the back at Randwick. First time around Eagle Farm.
runners.owner: Lees Racing Pty Ltd, P B Jones Et Al

races.race_name: Gran Premio Estrellas Classic - Copa Escribano Hernan Ceriani Cernadas Stakes (Group 1) (Turf)
Gran Premio Estrellas Mile - Auspicia Laboratorio Cimol Stakes (Group 1) (Turf)
Gran Premio Estrellas Distaff - Copa Senor Alejandro J Menditeguy (Group 1) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Hinata Grey
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred filly who won a 6f conditions race from a wayward Scapin on debut at Chantilly (very soft) having gone off a 9-1 chance; needs to cope with this much faster ground but has lots of potential for better.
runners.comment: Beat the wayward Scapin on debut and open to plenty of improvement

runners.name: Scapin
runners.spotlight: Runner-up both starts under this rider, including when behind the debuting Hinta Grey last time at Chantilly over 6f on very soft, looking really green late on while in front; no surprise blinkers go on, so may find some improvement for that headgear.
runners.comment: Runner-up both starts, looking a bit wayward last time; blinkers should help

runners.name: Tokaido
runners.spotlight: Looks the one to beat on what he's done so far, following his debut second with two wins, most recently a 5f Listed event at Chantilly in comfortable style (Aralia and Yuquot behind); pedigree suggests another furlong won't be an issue, so should be involved in the finish.
runners.comment: Beat two of these last time in a 5f Listed event; sound claims if effective at this trip

runners.name: Aralia
runners.spotlight: Only ever run over 5f at Chantilly; 5l behind Tokaido on her debut before winning a conditions event by 3l; followed that with a 1.5l defeat by Tokaido again in Listed company, so a little bit of work to do with that rival; having her first go over 6f.
runners.comment: Twice behind Tokaido in three starts, but run well and entitled to be thereabouts
runners.owner: Thierry Storme, Annua Racing SL et al

runners.name: Yuquot
runners.spotlight: Supplemented for this; won over 5.5f at Nantes on very soft on debut before finishing sixth behind both Tokaido and Aralia in a 5f good ground Listed event at Chantilly last month as a 16-1 chance; needs new trip to bring about improvement; retained jockey Maxime Guyon on for first time.
runners.comment: Behind both Tokaido and Aralia in a 5f Listed event 27 days ago; new trip nees to help

runners.name: Rouhi
runners.spotlight: Made a successful debut over 5.5f at Chantilly last month on good ground in a newcomers maiden (sent off 11-1), with that form relatively untested since; the betting will likely be the best guide to what's expected in this.
runners.comment: Won on debut over 5.5f at Chantilly; market will prove best guide to his chance
runners.owner: Ecurie Elag, RPG Bloodstock et al

races.race_name: Al Nujaifi Racing Trophy (Listed Race) (Pure-bred Arabians)

runners.name: Fareedhat El Izz (Fr)
runners.spotlight: Rattled off a quick hat trick last season including when winning this race by a wide margin; has joined a new yard since but she goes well fresh and is very well in at the weights here; Providing she's fit enough she's the one to beat. (R14 W4 P5) (OR 87)

runners.name: Hibikinada Du Mazet (Fr)
runners.spotlight: Finished in front of Fareedhat El Izz at Lingfield last season but that was in handicap company, and they meet on very different terms here; shaped as though the run was needed on her first start of this season for a new yard, her prominent style of racing should be suited to the track and if the likely favourite under performs she seems the most likely to take advantage, with blinkers going on for the first time. (R9 W1 P5) (OR 69)

runners.name: Ghayah (Fr)
runners.spotlight: Half Sister to three previous winners, all who where placed in pattern company; interesting that connections pitch her in at this level and the market will tell more; visored for her debut. (R0 W0 P0) (NOR)

runners.name: Sassy (Gb)
runners.spotlight: Has been a real credit to connections over the years winning twice last year after finishing fourth in this race last year; ran as well as could have been expected from a long way out of the weights at Bath last time out and holds place claims. (R16 W3 P7) (OR 56)

runners.name: Victoria (Gb)
runners.spotlight: Has only two wins to her name but she's been largely consistent in her career and ran a fine race to be second in this race last year; bumped into an improver on her seasonal return over further when she had three of these behind. Another who likes to be ridden prominently and hold place claims again. (R19 W2 P11) (OR 55)

runners.name: Zizania Du Mazet (Fr)
runners.spotlight: Has yet to add to her first win in her Juvenile season but did run well here over further last season when runner-up; as work to do on her two runs this season, but a stronger pace here might help as she stays well. (R10 W1 P6) (OR 52)

runners.name: Pharitz Lahia (Gb)
runners.spotlight: Maiden, who's best run came in this race last year when she finished a remote third; her two runs this season haven't been without promise and if she gets a strong pace to chase again, she's capable of hitting the frame again with cheekpieces on for the first time. (R9 W0 P2) (OR 41)

runners.name: Hattie Sparks (Gb)
runners.spotlight: Went close to shedding the maiden tag on her penultimate outing behind Sassy but didn't back that up when last seen; has joined a new yard since and for all she's open to improvement she has a lot to do at the weights. (R8 W0 P4) (OR 45)
